X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

schema.description: The number of requests left for the time window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

schema.description: The UTC date/time at which the current rate limit window resets.

delete_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
delete_device_function.name: device-function

delete_object_type.description: LOCAL - To delete local objects. SHARED - To delete shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
delete_object_type.name: object-type

delete_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be deleted. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
delete_scope_id.name: scope-id

detailed.description: True - Includes annotations in the returned json to indicate the type of object, scope, and device function. False - Returns only config objects without annotations.
detailed.name: detailed

effective.description: True - Returns effective configuration (i.e hierarchically merged configuration). False - Returns committed configuration at a given scope.
effective.name: effective

get_device_function.description: Filters configuration specific to given device-function type. If not provided and view_type = LOCAL, then defaults to all device-function's.
get_device_function.name: device-function

get_object_type.description: Retrieves LOCAL or SHARED or both(default) configuration objects.
get_object_type.name: object-type

get_scope_id.description: Configuration at a given scope. Mandatory for view-type = LOCAL.
get_scope_id.name: scope-id

patch_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
patch_device_function.name: device-function

patch_object_type.description: LOCAL - To update local objects. SHARED - To update shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
patch_object_type.name: object-type

patch_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be updated. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
patch_scope_id.name: scope-id

post_device_function.description: Device function for which the local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object_type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object_type = SHARED.
post_device_function.name: device-function

post_object_type.description: LOCAL - To create local objects. SHARED - To create shared objects. Default - Defaults to SHARED if not provided
post_object_type.name: object-type

post_scope_id.description: Scope at which local object needs to be created. Mandatory if object-type = LOCAL. Should not be provided for object-type = SHARED.
post_scope_id.name: scope-id

view_type.description: LIBRARY - Returns shared objects in library. For LIBRARY, all other parameters listed below, except 'detailed' , will be ignored. LOCAL - Returns configurations for a scope and one or more device function based on parameters below.
view_type.name: view-type

aaa-bw-contract.description:
AAA bandwidth contract assignments - Associates a named AAA
bandwidth contract with a traffic direction and an optional association
scope (per-user or per-apgroup) within a role or interface. At most one contract can
be assigned per direction.

bw-contract.description:
List of bandwidth contract assignments keyed by traffic
direction. Each entry assigns a named AAA bandwidth contract to either
upstream or downstream traffic, with an optional
association scope of per-user or per-apgroup.

x-enumDescriptions.PER_APGROUP: Assign bandwidth contract per-apgroup (default is per-role).
x-enumDescriptions.PER_USER: Assign bandwidth contract per-user (default is per-role).

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

aaa-bw-contract.description:
AAA bandwidth contract assignments - Associates a named AAA
bandwidth contract with a traffic direction and an optional association
scope (per-user or per-apgroup) within a role or interface. At most one contract can
be assigned per direction.

bw-contract.description:
List of bandwidth contract assignments keyed by traffic
direction. Each entry assigns a named AAA bandwidth contract to either
upstream or downstream traffic, with an optional
association scope of per-user or per-apgroup.

x-enumDescriptions.PER_APGROUP: Assign bandwidth contract per-apgroup (default is per-role).
x-enumDescriptions.PER_USER: Assign bandwidth contract per-user (default is per-role).

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

app-aaa-contract.description:
Application-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA bandwidth
contracts with specific DPI applications and a traffic direction, allowing
per-application rate limiting.

app.description:
List of application-based bandwidth contracts keyed by application name
and traffic direction. Each entry maps a specific DPI application
(e.g. facebook, youtube, zoom) to a named AAA bandwidth contract for
either upstream or downstream traffic.

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

app-aaa-contract.description:
Application-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA bandwidth
contracts with specific DPI applications and a traffic direction, allowing
per-application rate limiting.

app.description:
List of application-based bandwidth contracts keyed by application name
and traffic direction. Each entry maps a specific DPI application
(e.g. facebook, youtube, zoom) to a named AAA bandwidth contract for
either upstream or downstream traffic.

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

ArubaAaaBandwidthContract_AppCatBasedAaa.description: App category based AAA Bandwidth contracts.

app-category-aaa-contract.description:
Application category-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA
bandwidth contracts with DPI application categories and a traffic direction,
allowing rate limiting to be applied at the category level rather than
per individual application.

app-category.description:
List of application category-based bandwidth contracts keyed by
category name and traffic direction. Each entry maps a DPI application
category (e.g. STREAMING, GAMING, SOCIAL-NETWORKING) to a named AAA
bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic.

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

ArubaAaaBandwidthContract_AppCatBasedAaa.description: App category based AAA Bandwidth contracts.

app-category-aaa-contract.description:
Application category-based AAA bandwidth contracts. Associates named AAA
bandwidth contracts with DPI application categories and a traffic direction,
allowing rate limiting to be applied at the category level rather than
per individual application.

app-category.description:
List of application category-based bandwidth contracts keyed by
category name and traffic direction. Each entry maps a DPI application
category (e.g. STREAMING, GAMING, SOCIAL-NETWORKING) to a named AAA
bandwidth contract for either upstream or downstream traffic.

x-enumDescriptions.DOWNSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to downstream traffic.
x-enumDescriptions.UPSTREAM: Assign bandwidth contract to upstream traffic.

exclude-app-contract.description:
Exclude specific DPI applications from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Applications listed here will bypass bandwidth contract rate limiting
even if a matching contract is configured.

exclude-app.description:
List of DPI applications excluded from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Each entry specifies an application name that will bypass any configured
bandwidth contract rate limits.

exclude-app-contract.description:
Exclude specific DPI applications from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Applications listed here will bypass bandwidth contract rate limiting
even if a matching contract is configured.

exclude-app.description:
List of DPI applications excluded from bandwidth contract enforcement.
Each entry specifies an application name that will bypass any configured
bandwidth contract rate limits.

exclude-app-cat-contract.description:
Exclude specific DPI application categories from bandwidth contract
enforcement. Application categories listed here will bypass bandwidth
contract rate limiting even if a matching contract is configured.

exclude-app-category.description:
List of DPI application categories excluded from bandwidth contract
enforcement. Each entry specifies a category name (e.g. STREAMING,
GAMING, SOCIAL-NETWORKING) whose traffic will bypass any configured
bandwidth contract rate limits.

exclude-app-cat-contract.description:
Exclude specific DPI application categories from bandwidth contract
enforcement. Application categories listed here will bypass bandwidth
contract rate limiting even if a matching contract is configured.

exclude-app-category.description:
List of DPI application categories excluded from bandwidth contract
enforcement. Each entry specifies a category name (e.g. STREAMING,
GAMING, SOCIAL-NETWORKING) whose traffic will bypass any configured
bandwidth contract rate limits.

eap-type.description: EAP method, default method is EAP-PEAP.

enable.description: Enable 802.1X authentication to be terminated on the controller. This option is disabled by default

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_GTC: EAP-GenericTokenCard as the inner authentication protocol.
x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MSCHAPV2: EAP-MSCHAPV2 as the inner authentication protocol.

eap-type.description: EAP method, default method is EAP-PEAP.

enable.description: Enable 802.1X authentication to be terminated on the controller. This option is disabled by default

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_GTC: EAP-GenericTokenCard as the inner authentication protocol.
x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MSCHAPV2: EAP-MSCHAPV2 as the inner authentication protocol.

auth-vlan-id.description:
Configures the VLAN where the port is moved after a successful
authentication.
auth-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ca-cert.description: CA Certificate Name for the Client Certificate Verification.

cached-reauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) during which cached re-authentication
is allowed. This is applicable only when cached-reauth is enabled.

canned-eap-success-enable.description:
Allow the device to send a canned EAP success packet to the supplicant
to indicate a successful authentication when:
1. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server reject, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a reject-role
configured.
2. The authentication fails due to a RADIUS server timeout, but
the interface on which the client on-boarded has a critical-role
configured.

cert-cn-lookup.description: Check certificate common name against AAA server.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTHORIZED:
Gives access to a device connected to the port even without a valid
802.1X crendential.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO:
The device connected to the port must support 802.1X authentication
and provide valid credentials to get network access.
x-enumDescriptions.UNAUTHORIZED:
Deny access to a device connected to the port even with a valid
802.1X crendential.

delete-keycache.description: Delete key cache entry when user entry is deleted.

discovery-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before an EAPoL request
identity frame re-transmission on an 802.1X enabled interface
with no authenticated client.

eapol-max-requests.description:
The number of EAPOL requests to send to a supplicant
that must time out before authentication fails and
the authentication session ends.

eapol-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a response from a client before
retransmitting an EAPoL PDU.

enforce-suite-b-128.description: Enable Suite-B 128 bit or more security level Authentication enforcement.

enforce-suite-b-192.description: Enable Suite-B 192 bit or more security level Authentication enforcement.

heldstate-bypass.description: Maximum number of times station can send bad credentials

initial-auth-response-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for the first EAPoL frame from a
client before deeming the client to be incapable of 802.1X and
start authentication using the next configured authentication
method, if any.
If not set, the next configured authentication method is attempted only
after the authentication cycle of the current method is complete.

keycache-timeout.description: Per BSSID PMKSA (Pairwise Master Key Security Association) cache interval in hours.

logoff-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) after which a client will be considered
removed from the port for a lack of activity.

macsec.description:
Enables the switch to provision a MACsec channel
dynamically when the 802.1X client is authenticated using
an EAP method that supports mutual authentication.

mka-cak-length.description: Length of CAK to generate for MKA.
mka-cak-length.x-typeDescription: Secure Connectivity Association Key (CAK) length type.

max-reauth.description: Max number of reauthentication attempts before giving up.

max-retries.description:
Number of authentication attempts before authentication fails.
GW supported max range is 5

multicast-key-rotation-period.description: Set the period between multicast key rotation.

multicast-keyrotation.description: Enable or Disable Multicast Key Rotation.

opp-key-caching.description:
Enable Opportunistic Key Caching (WPA2/WPA3 Only).By default, the 802.1X authentication profile enables a
cached pairwise master key (PMK) derived via a client and
an associated AP and used when the client roams to a new AP.
This allows clients faster roaming without a full 802.1x authentication.

quiet-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before processing an authentication
request from a client that failed authentication.

reauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to enforce periodic re-authentication of
clients. This is applicable only when reauth is enabled

reauth-server-termination-action.description: Use the termination-action attribute from the Server.

server-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a server response to an
authentication request. If there is no response within the configured
time frame, the device assumes that the authentication attempt has
timed out.

tls-guest-access.description: Enable guest access for users with valid certificate.

unauth-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait before moving the interface to the
VLAN for unauthenticated clients.

unauth-vlan-id.description:
Configures the VLAN where the port is moved with an unauthenticated
client.
unauth-vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

unicast-key-rotation-period.description: Set the period between unicast key rotation.

unicast-keyrotation.description: Enable or Disable Unicast Key Rotation.

use-session-key.description: Use RADIUS Session Key as the unicast WEP key. This option is disabled by default.

use-static-key.description: Use Static Key as the Unicast/Multicast WEP key.This option is disabled by default.

validate-pmkid.description:
PMK (Pairwise Master Key) identifier (WPA2/WPA3 Only).
This parameter instructs the controller to check the pairwise master key (PMK) ID
sent by the client. When this option is enabled,
the client must send a PMKID in the associate or reassociate frame to indicate that it supports OKC or PMK caching;
otherwise, full 802.1x authentication takes place.

wep-key-retries.description: Set the Number of times Unicast/Multicast EAPOL Key Messages are sent to the station.

wep-key-size.description: Set the Dynamic WEP Key Size.

wpa-groupkey-delay.description: Delay between WPA unicast and multicast key exchange.

wpa-key-period.description: Set the time period between each WPA/WPA2/WPA3 key exchange.

wpa-key-retries.description: Set the Number of times WPA/WPA2/WPA3 Key Messages are retried.

wpa2-key-delay.description: Delay between EAP-Success and unicast key exchange.

denylist-on-failure.description: Add the station to denylist if machine authentication fails.

machine-default-role.description: Default role assigned on only completing Machine Authentication.

user-default-role.description: Configure the Role assigned on only completing User Authentication.

denylist-on-failure.description: Add the station to denylist if machine authentication fails.

machine-default-role.description: Default role assigned on only completing Machine Authentication.

user-default-role.description: Configure the Role assigned on only completing User Authentication.

canned-eap-success.description:
Configure the device to accept an EAP success without going through
the complete authentication cycle.

discovery-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a potential 802.1X authenticator
on the other end before considering the link to be non 802.1X capable
and opening the interface on the data-plane.
On a timeout, the device will not use the authentication result to
determine the forwarding behavior of the interface until a link flap.
If not configured, the device will wait for the 802.1X authentication
cycle to complete before determining the forwarding state of the
interface.

eapol-force-multicast.description:
Force the device to send only multicast EAPoL packets irrespective of
receiving unicast EAPoL packets from the authenticator.

eapol-method.description: EAP method to use for authentication.

x-enumDescriptions.EAP_MD5: MD5 hash function to generate hashed password

eapol-protocol-version.description:
EAPoL protocol version to use in EAPoL frames transmitted by the
device.

eapol-source-mac.description: The MAC address to use as source MAC in EAPoL frames.

x-enumDescriptions.INTERFACE_MAC:
Use the MAC address of the interface on which 802.1X is running as
the source MAC in EAPoL frames.
x-enumDescriptions.SYSTEM_MAC:
Use the MAC address of the device on which 802.1X is running as
the source MAC in EAPoL frames.

eapol-timeout.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait for a response from an authenticator
before reattempting authentication.

fail-mode.description:
Forwarding behavior of the interface when the 802.1X authentication
fails.

x-enumDescriptions.FAIL_CLOSED: Block the interface on the data-plane if the authentication fails.
x-enumDescriptions.FAIL_OPEN:
Open the interface on the data-plane even if the authentication
fails.

held-period.description:
Time period (in seconds) to wait after a failed
authentication attempt before another attempt is permitted.

mka-cak-length.description: Length of CAK to generate for MKA.
mka-cak-length.x-typeDescription: Secure Connectivity Association Key (CAK) length type.

policy.description: MACsec policy to use for EAP MACsec.

max-retries.description:
Maximum number of authentication attempts before authentication
fails.

start-mode.description:
Forwarding behavior of the interface when the 802.1X supplicant starts
operation.

x-enumDescriptions.START_CLOSED:
Block the interface on the data-plane until the authentication is
successful.
x-enumDescriptions.START_OPEN:
Open the interface on the data-plane before the authentication is
successful.

eap-identity-ciphertext-password.description: Ciphertext password associated with the EAP identity.

eap-identity-password.description: Plaintext password associated with the EAP identity.
eap-identity-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

eap-identity-ciphertext-password.description: Ciphertext password associated with the EAP identity.

eap-identity-password.description: Plaintext password associated with the EAP identity.
eap-identity-password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

addr-limit.description: Maximum number of authenticated mac addresses that can be allowed on the interface.

address-format.description:
The MAC address format to be used in the RADIUS
request messages for authentication.

Configures the VLAN where the port is moved after a successful
authentication.

case-type.description:
The case for MAC address format to be used in the
RADIUS request messages for authentication.

enable.description: Enable MAC authentication on a port.

Time period (in seconds) after which a client will be considered

unknown unicast) out authentication enabled interfaces with no
authenticated client.

items.description:
Precedence order for port-access authentication. Auth-precedence must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If empty, the default precedence
order of dot1x followed by mac-auth will be used.
items.x-typeDescription: Authentication methods.

items.description:
Priority order for port-access authentication. Auth-priority must
contain either 0 or 2 elements. If not set, priority will follow
the 'auth-precedence' order.
items.x-typeDescription: Authentication methods.

items.description:
The list of reasons when a client can be automatically logged off by
the device.

x-enumDescriptions.FINAL_AUTHENTICATION_FAILURE:
The device automatically logs-off a client if it fails
authentication with all the enabled authentication methods.
The authentication failure reason should not be one of server-reject
or server-timeout server-reject or server-timeout.

items.x-typeDescription: Client auto logff reasons.

x-enumDescriptions.ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces without authentication itself
on the new interface.
x-enumDescriptions.SECURE_ENABLED:
Client can move between interfaces but only after authenticating
itself on the new interface.

concurrent-onboarding.description:
Enable authentication methods to start concurrently for faster
onboarding. If not set, clients are onboarded based on the order
specified by 'onboarding-precedence'.

controlled-direction.description:
Configure how traffic is controlled on non-authenticated ports: in both directions (ingress and egress) or ingress only.
Default is BOTH.

x-enumDescriptions.BOTH: Exert access control in both directions.
x-enumDescriptions.IN: Exert access control on incoming packets only.

controlled-direction.x-typeDescription: Controlled direction mode for port-access authentication.

interface when multi-domain is enabled. If the number of 'data' clients
on the interface exceeds this client limit, it will be considered as a
violation and the configured threshold-violation action is taken.

ArubaAaaProfile_AuthenticationLagAttribu.description: Group for authentication attributes which are supported on lag interface.

auth-mode.description:
The mode determining how each client on the interface can gain access
to the network.

x-enumDescriptions.CLIENT_MODE:
Each client must be authenticated separately to be granted network
access.
x-enumDescriptions.DEVICE_MODE:
The first authenticated client grants network access to all the other
clients.
x-enumDescriptions.MULTI_DOMAIN:
Limit the number of authenticated clients to one 'voice' client and a
configured number of 'data' clients.

cdp.description: Enable CDP packets to trigger authentication.

lldp.description: Enable LLDP packets to trigger authentication.

mac-type.description:
MAC address to use when authentication is triggered by an
LLDP frame. This is applicable only when LLDP packets are
allowed to trigger authentication.

x-enumDescriptions.CHASSIS_MAC: Use the chassis MAC in LLDP TLV
x-enumDescriptions.SOURCE_MAC: Use the source MAC in LLDP frame

bpdu-bypass.description:
BPDUs to be serviced by the device before authentication is
successful on the interface.

cdp.description:
Enable CDP packets to be serviced by the device before
authentication is successful on the interface.

lldp.description:
Enable LLDP packets to be serviced by the device before
authentication is successful on the interface.

bpdu-proxy-logoff.description:
Proxy logoff the client based on the interface status in BPDU packets.
Device will clear authentication session of the client if it receives
an interface down status in the BPDU packet.
When not configured, no action is taken.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

client-limit.description: Maximum number of clients that can be authenticated on the interface.

auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that have been authenticated
but not received any specific role or attribute from the authentication
server.
When not set, no role is applied for clients that successfully
authenticated without a role assigned from the RADIUS server.

auto-vlan-enable.description:
Enable automatic creation of a VLAN for authenticated clients if the
VLAN is not already available on the device.

cached-critical-role-enable.description:
Enables caching authenticated clients and their associated
role information on the interface. Clients are assigned to their
previously authenticated role.

critical-auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients, that failed authentication
due to unreachable authentication servers.
When not set, no role is applied for clients that could not
complete authentication due to RADIUS server being unreachable.

critical-data-vlan.description: Configure critical-auth VLAN as data VLAN.
critical-data-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

critical-voice-role.description:
The role to be applied to 'voice' client that have failed
re-authentication due to servers being unreachable.
When not set, no role will be applied for voice clients when they
fail re-authentication due to server being unreachable.

critical-voice-vlan.description: Configure critical-auth VLAN as voice VLAN.
critical-voice-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

x-enumDescriptions.NAMED_VLAN: Configure the VLAN(s) using a named-VLAN
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN_RANGES: Configure the VLAN(s) as a list of VLAN ranges

fallback-role.description:
The role that will be applied to clients that have not been
assigned a role via any of the onboarding methods.
When not set, no role is applied for the client.

open-data-vlan.description: Configure open-auth VLAN as data VLAN.
open-data-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

open-voice-vlan.description: Configure open-auth VLAN as voice VLAN.
open-voice-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

pre-auth-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that are yet to be authenticated.
When not set, no role is applied for clients while the
authentication is in progress for the client.

radius-override-enable.description:
Enable RADIUS override support. When enabled, RADIUS attributes
received from the server will override corresponding attributes
configured in the role assigned to the client.

reject-role.description:
The role to be applied for clients that failed authentication.
When not set, for AOS-CX devices, no role is applied for clients that fail authentication.
A default denyall role is applied on AOS-S devices.

ubt-fallback-role.description:
For any client,for which the corresponding UBT zone reachability
is failed, ubt-fallback role is applied. The client on ubt-fallback
role is automatically reverted to the previous role whenever UBT zone
status is back to reachable state.

ArubaAaaProfile_SecurityViolationConfigG.description: Group for security violation configurations shared by aaa-profile and port-security.

action.description:
Action to take on a security violation (notify or shutdown).
This is only supported on physical ports.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Prevent an SNMP trap from being sent.
x-enumDescriptions.NOTIFY: Send an SNMP trap and log an event on a violation.
x-enumDescriptions.SHUTDOWN:
Send an SNMP trap, log an event and shut the interface down on a
violation.

recovery-timer.description:
Time in seconds after which the interface is re-enabled if it was
shutdown in response to a violation event.
This is only applicable if shutdown recovery is enabled.

shutdown-recovery-enable.description:
Enable auto-recovery of the interface. This is only applicable when
the violation action is set to shutdown.
The interface is re-enabled after the recovery timer has expired.

ArubaApPortProfile_ApPortProfilesSchemaG.description: Port profiles, config parameters on the port such as VLAN settings, authentication methods, link speed, PoE settings, and more.

profile.description: List of Port profile, config parameters on the port such as VLAN settings, authentication methods, link speed, PoE settings, and more.

client-isolation.description: Disable all peer-to-peer communication in the VLAN network.

description.description: User-defined description or comment for the Port Profile.

forward-mode.description: Forwarding mode for the network on AP.
forward-mode.x-typeDescription: Forwarding mode for the network on AP

inactivity-timeout.description: Specify an interval for a client session timeout in seconds.

loop-protect.description: Config loop protection on the port.

enable.description: Enables loop protect on the L2 interface.

re-enable.description:
If the Ethernet port of AP is marked with status Loop-ERR and shutdown, at the
expiry of the auto-recovery-interval, the Loop-ERR status of the Ethernet port
will be cleared and the port will be re-enabled automatically.

re-enable-timer.description: Time interval for enabling port blocked by loop-protect.

storm-control-broadcast.description:
During broadcast-storm control, AP counts the broadcast packets received
on each of its Ethernet port and determines the packet rate in an interval.
If the broadcast packet rate on one Ethernet port exceeds the configured
threshold (default value is 2000 packets per second), the Ethernet port
is shut down.

storm-control-threshold.description:
The threshhold is used for storm-control-broadcast, if the broadcast packet
rate on one Ethernet port exceeds the threshold (default value is 2000
packets per second), the Ethernet port will be shut down.

port-bond.description: Bond this Ethernet port into virtual port bond0 as uplink.

port-type.description: Port works in WAN or LAN.

stp.description: Config Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) on the port, Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) is a network protocol used to prevent loops in Ethernet networks.

ArubaApPortProfile_WiredPortVlanConfig.description: Config VLAN parameters on an port, such like trunk or access, native VLAN ID, allowed VLANs.

access-vlan.description: Assign the access VLAN to the access port.
access-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

allowed-vlan-name.description: Named-VLAN to map to this port.

allowed-vlan-selector.description:
Method to specify the VLAN(s) for this port:
If it is 'VLAN_RANGES', it can be a combination of
list of VLAN-ID-ranges and list of VLAN-IDs
Example: 61-65,67,69,71-75.

x-enumDescriptions.NAMED_VLAN: Configure the VLAN(s) using a named-VLAN
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN_RANGES: Configure the VLAN(s) as a list of VLAN ranges

interface-mode.description: Interface access or trunk mode for VLANs.

native-vlan.description:
Native VLAN id for untagged frames arriving on
a trunk interface.  Tagged frames sent on an interface
configured with a native VLAN should have their tags
stripped prior to transmission. This configuration is only
valid on a trunk interface.
native-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

trunk-vlan-all.description: Allow all vlans for trunk mode interface.

items.description: List of VLANs or VLAN ranges to map to this port.
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

Example: 61-65,67,69,71-75.

stripped prior to transmission. This configuration is only
valid on a trunk interface.

VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

domain-name.x-typeDescription:
The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

lte-modem.description:
Uplink LTE Modem, this feature is used to configure modem parameters
for 3G or 4G uplink and only applies to AP, the 3G and 4G modems can be used to extend connectivity to places
and enables the AP to automatically choose the available network in a specific region.
This feature supports configuring both 3rd party modem and aruba modem, but only one modem can be used as the uplink.

wifi-uplink.description:
Wifi uplink, this feature is used to configure ARUBA AP as a standard WiFi client to assoc with Aruba ap or third party devices.
When both sides are Aruba AP, Aruba Mesh should be the first choice.

ArubaApUplink_CellSimPin.description:
AP uplink cellular sim pin is a security method to protect the SIM card from unauthorized access,
the following operations can be configured for the sim pin:
1.sim-pin-enable: enable SIM PIN lock
2.sim-pin-renew: update the current SIM PIN
3.sim-pin-puk: unlock the SIM PIN using PUK.

sim-pin-enable.description: Enables locking of the SIM PIN, to enable SIM PIN lock, the PIN code should be same as the PIN code that is currently used.

current.description: Current PIN of the modem SIM.

sim-pin-puk.description: Unlocks the SIM PIN using the PUK code provided by the ISP and by entering a new PIN code.

sim-pin-renew.description: Renews the SIM PIN of the modem, update the current SIM PIN by entering a new SIM PIN.

current.description: Current PIN of the modem SIM.

new.description: New SIM PIN for the modem.

ArubaApUplink_CellSimPinGet.description:
AP uplink cellular sim pin is a security method to protect the SIM card from unauthorized access,
the following operations can be configured for the sim pin:

sim-pin-enable.description: Enables locking of the SIM PIN, to enable SIM PIN lock, the PIN code should be same as the PIN code that is currently used.

current.description: Current PIN of the modem SIM.

sim-pin-puk.description: Unlocks the SIM PIN using the PUK code provided by the ISP and by entering a new PIN code.

sim-pin-renew.description: Renews the SIM PIN of the modem, update the current SIM PIN by entering a new SIM PIN.

current.description: Current PIN of the modem SIM.

new.description: New SIM PIN for the modem.

enable.description:
Enables pre-emption when no uplinks are enforced. When enabled, if the current uplink is active,
the AP periodically tries to use a higher priority uplink, and switches to a higher priority uplink even if the current uplink is active.

customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

x-enumDescriptions.MPLS: WAN circuit type is MultiProtocol Label Switching.

wan-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

enable.description:
Enables uplink switchover based on the availability of the Internet. When enabled, the
AP continuously sends ICMP packets to some well-known Internet servers. If the request
is timed out due to a bad uplink connection or uplink interface failure, and the public
Internet is not reachable from the current uplink, the AP switches to a different connection.

ip.description: IP address to which the ICMP or UDP Probe packets are sent in the event of Internet failure.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

The domain-name type represents a DNS domain name.
Fully quallified left to the models which utilize this type.

Internet domain names are only loosely specified.  Section
3.5 of RFC 1034 recommends a syntax (modified in Section
2.1 of RFC 1123).  The pattern above is intended to allow
for current practice in domain name use, and some possible
future expansion.  It is designed to hold various types of
domain names, including names used for A or AAAA records
(host names) and other records, such as SRV records.  Note
that Internet host names have a stricter syntax (described
in RFC 952) than the DNS recommendations in RFCs 1034 and
1123, and that systems that want to store host names in
schema nodes using the domain-name type are recommended to
adhere to this stricter standard to ensure interoperability.

The encoding of DNS names in the DNS protocol is limited
to 255 characters.  Since the encoding consists of labels
prefixed by a length bytes and there is a trailing NULL
byte, only 253 characters can appear in the textual dotted
notation.

Domain-name values use the US-ASCII encoding.  Their canonical
format uses lowercase US-ASCII characters.  Internationalized
domain names MUST be encoded in punycode as described in RFC
3492

ip-for-cell.description: Configures the Internet failover IP address for a cellular 3G/4G uplink.

called-station-id.description:
Configure this parameter to be sent with the RADIUS attribute Called Station ID for authentication and accounting requests. For AP, this API is applicable for
WLAN profile and AP port profile. For GW, this API is applicable for the authentication server profile. This feature is applicable for AP and GW.

include-ssid-delimiter.description: The delimiter between mac address and SSID. For example, the delimiter is dash. The called-station-id like aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff-wlanssid.

mac-address-delimiter.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string and no delimiter, when configure mac-address-delimiter, AP MAC Address will be added delimiter in authentication packet.

type.description:
Configure the called-station-id types.
For AP, The type includes AP group, AP MAC address, AP name, AP IP or user vlan. The default value is AP MAC address.

upper-case.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string, when enabled upper-case, AP MAC Address will be changed to upper case in authentication packet.

called-station-id.description:
Configure this parameter to be sent with the RADIUS attribute Called Station ID for authentication and accounting requests. For AP, this API is applicable for
WLAN profile and AP port profile. For GW, this API is applicable for the authentication server profile. This feature is applicable for AP and GW.

include-ssid-delimiter.description: The delimiter between mac address and SSID. For example, the delimiter is dash. The called-station-id like aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff-wlanssid.

mac-address-delimiter.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string and no delimiter, when configure mac-address-delimiter, AP MAC Address will be added delimiter in authentication packet.

type.description:
Configure the called-station-id types.
For AP, The type includes AP group, AP MAC address, AP name, AP IP or user vlan. The default value is AP MAC address.

upper-case.description: By default, called-station-id is AP MAC Address string, when enabled upper-case, AP MAC Address will be changed to upper case in authentication packet.

x-enumDescriptions.RX_ONLY: Enable receive only mode of CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) processing

ArubaCellular_ArubaModemConfig.description: Cellular uplink configurations for Aruba modem.

aruba-skylark.description: Enables the pre-configured profile for Aruba Skylark LTE modems. When enabled, this creates and activates the ARUBA_USB_LTE profile optimized for Skylark hardware and disables configurations for other modem types. Gateway devices only.

auto-upgrade.description: Automatic firmware upgrade for Aruba modem, this API is AP specific and only for Aruba modem.

billing-start-date.description: The day of the month when the cellular billing cycle begins. Used in conjunction with data-rate-limit to track monthly data usage. The data usage counter resets on this date each month.

data-rate-limit.description: The maximum monthly data usage limit for the cellular connection in megabits per second.

frequency.description:
Cellular frequency band configuration for the modem. Groups generation (3G/4G) and
frequency-range settings that together configure the cellular modem's frequency band.

frequency-range.description: The specific radio frequency band to use for cellular communication. Restricting to specific bands can optimize connectivity in regions where certain frequencies have better coverage or performance. Used in conjunction with generation parameter.

generation.description: The cellular technology generation (3G or 4G) used to filter available frequency bands. Used in conjunction with frequency-range to specify precise radio frequency bands for the modem.

gps.description: Enable the GPS on the Aruba modem

internal-lte.description: Enable or disable internal LTE modem. When true, enables internal-lte. When false, disables internal-lte.

modem-apn.description:
Modem APN(Access Point Name) Configuration is used by modem to connect to a mobile network
for internet access, this configuration help the modem identify the correct network and establish a data connection.

apn.description: The Access Point Name (APN) provided by your mobile carrier to establish the data connection. The APN identifies the packet data network (PDN) that the modem should connect to. Valid only when using Aruba modems.

apn-authentication.description: The authentication protocol used to verify credentials with the mobile carrier. Valid only when APN password is configured. Supported values: 0 (No authentication), 1 (PAP - Password Authentication Protocol, sends credentials in clear text), 2 (CHAP - Challenge Handshake Authentication Protocol, uses hashed credentials), 3 (MSCHAPv2 - Microsoft CHAP version 2, enhanced security).

modem-e3372.description: Enables the pre-configured profile optimized for Huawei E3372h-320 USB LTE modems. When enabled, the system applies modem-specific initialization and connection parameters. Gateway devices only.

network-mode.description: The preferred cellular network generation for connection. AUTO allows the modem to select the best available technology (3G/4G), while fixed modes (MODE_3G, MODE_4G) restrict connectivity to a specific generation. Forcing a specific mode can improve stability in areas with weak higher-generation signals. Valid only when using Aruba modems. Aruba modems support AUTO, MODE_3G, and MODE_4G.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: the network mode for aruba modem is automatic.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOM: Custom mode used by Central UI. Not used for CLI configuration.
x-enumDescriptions.MODE_3G: the network mode for aruba modem is 3G.
x-enumDescriptions.MODE_4G: the network mode for aruba modem is 4G.

network-mode.x-typeDescription: the cellular network mode for Aruba modem.

plmn.description: The Public Land Mobile Network (PLMN) identifier used to manually select a specific mobile operator network. PLMN consists of Mobile Country Code (MCC) and Mobile Network Code (MNC). Valid only when using Aruba modems. Accepts 5-6 digits for AP, GW.

prefer-modem.description: The preferred modem, this API is AP specific and only for Aruba modem.

x-enumDescriptions.NONE: when prefer-modem config is not required.

sim-failover.description: Enables automatic failover between SIM card slots when the active SIM loses connectivity. When enabled, the system will switch to the alternate SIM slot if the primary connection fails, ensuring continuous cellular uplink availability. Applicable to 9004-LTE only.

sim-slot.description: Selects the active SIM card slot (applicable for 9004-LTE only). Use SIM1 or SIM2 to specify which SIM card should be used for cellular connectivity.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: automatic selection of SIM slot.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: when SIM config is not required.

sim-slot.x-typeDescription: the SIM slot for Aruba modem.

tp-cell-auth.description:
Cellular authentication parameters,these parameters are important credentials used to verify user identity,
these parameters are typically provided by ISP to ensure that only authorized users can access the network.

password.description:
The password for the account associated with the subscriber of the selected Internet Service Provider,
this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.

username.description:

this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.

tp-device-id.description: The device ID of the modem, this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.

tp-device-tty.description: The modem TTY port, this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.

tp-dial-cmd.description: The parameter to dial the cell tower, this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.

tp-driver-type.description:
The device driver required for 3G or 4G modem, this API is AP specific and only for 3rd party modem.
Range: OPTION, ACM, AIRPRIME, HSO, SIERRA_EVDO, SIERRA_GSM, PANTECH_3G, ETHER_3G, SIERRA_NET,

ArubaDeviceProfile_DeviceProfileSchemaGE.description:
Device-Profile allows automatic discovery and configuration of other
devices such as APs, IP phones, Security cameras, Printers etc.. on the
network. The discovery is done via LLDP, CDP or MAC match.

criteria specified in this profile.

ArubaDevicefingerprintingInterface_Inter.description: Grouping for Port-level configuration parameters of Device Fingerprinting.

client-limit.description:
Specifiy Maximum number of clients allowed on the port/portlist for Device Fingerprinting.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

incoming-clients-only.description:
Enables fingerprinting for the clients that comes in after
enabling the fingerprinting feature on the port of AOS-S switch.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

profile-name.description:
Apply a Device Fingerprinting Profile to a port/portlist.
This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

ArubaDhcpClient_DhcpClientSchemaGET.description:
Dynamic Host Configuration Protocol Client Options Configuration.
The main purpose of this feature is to enable DHCP client on the
preferred VLAN. The DHCP client feature allows VLAN to act as a
host requesting configuration parameters such as IP address,
DNS IPs, ZTP information etc.

profile.description: Global profile for DHCP Client Option Configuration.

An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

ip-vrf.description: Key combination of ip and vrf separated by ~

zone.

assignment-rules.description:
Assign the client a user role, VLAN ID or VLAN name based on attributes. For GW and AP, the attributes is returned for the client by the authentication server and this API is applicable for
auth server group. For AP, this API is also applicable WLAN profile and if this API works in WLAN profile, the attributes can be returned for the client by the authentication server or other
attributes such as mac-address, dhcp-option, AP-Name, etc. Rules are ordered: the first rule that matches the configured condition is applied. VLAN IDs and VLAN names cannot be listed together.
This feature is only applicable for AP and GW.

attribute.description:
Attribute returned by the authentication server.
For AP, it also includes some custom attributes like mac-address, dhcp-option and fw_mode.

named-vlan.description: This is the assigned named VLAN when there is a rule match.

operand.description:
Match operand, For GW, the VSA value. For AP, it is VSA value under server group profile.
It is VSA value and mac-addrees, dhcp-option and fw_mode under SSID profile and AP wired port profile.

role.description: This is the assigned Role when there is a rule match.

sequence-id.description: The sequence id determines the rule order.

vlan.description: This is the assigned VLAN when there is a rule match.

Enables centralized Flow Telemetry.
This configuration point coordinates the activation of multiple telemetry features
(IPFIX, traffic insights) through a unified control mechanism.

ArubaGwPortProfile_GwPortProfilesSchemaG.description:
To configure and manage port profiles. A port-profile can be used to configure
ethernet interface. Same profile can be applied to multiple interfaces and
avoids repeating same configuration for interfaces. Gigabit ethernet config, LLDP,
spanning-tree, switch-port configuration, etc can be applied using the port-profile.

switchport.description: Switchport configuration. This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

access-vlan.description: Assign the access VLAN to the access port.
access-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

interface-mode.description:
Interface access or trunk mode for VLANs.
The access-vlan should be set to null for configuring trunk mode and
native-vlan and trunk-vlan-ranges should be set to null while configuring access mode
Eg: switchport: {interface-mode: ACCESS, access-vlan: 600, native-vlan: null,
trunk-vlan-ranges: null}
switchport: {interface-mode: TRUNK, access-vlan: null, native-vlan: NATIVE_VLAN,
trunk-vlan-ranges: [ 1000 ]}

native-vlan.description:
Native VLAN id for untagged frames arriving on
a trunk interface.  Tagged frames sent on an interface
configured with a native VLAN should have their tags
stripped prior to transmission. This configuration is only
valid on a trunk interface.
native-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: VLAN ranges that the interface may carry when in trunk mode.
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

broadcast-limit.description: Limit network bandwidth used by broadcast traffic

dpi-enable.description: Enable deep packet inspection (DPI) on an interface level.

energy-efficient.description:
Enable auto-negotiation of Energy-Efficient Ethernet (EEE). Supported primarily on 1000BASE-T ports
for 4100i, 5420, 6000, 6100, 6200, 6300 and 6400 platforms.

error-control.description:
Forward Error Correction (FEC). Add redundant bits to a data stream
enabling the receiver to detect and correct transmission errors.

fault-monitor-profile.description: Fault monitoring profile to be applied.

link-clock-narrow-tolerance.description:
Narrow clock tolerance for a port in 100 Mb/sec mode. Note that this feature only applies to Smart Rate ports
and only when linked at 100Mbps. It has no effect at other speeds or on other ports. Supported primarily on
Smartrate ports for 6200 and 6300 platforms

items.description: Define the events that the SNMP Trap will monitor. The configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

x-enumDescriptions.AGED: On the enabled port a MAC is aged out.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARNED: On this port a MAC learn occurs.
x-enumDescriptions.MOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is moved.
x-enumDescriptions.REMOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is removed.

items.x-typeDescription: SNMP trap events

macsec-policy.description: MACsec policy to use on the interface.

mka-policy.description: MKA policy to use on the interface.

mtu.description:
Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) of the interface in bytes. The MTU defines the largest packet size
that can be transmitted over the interface without fragmentation.

mvrp.description: MVRP configuration used with trunk interfaces.

pvlan-port-mode.description:
Port modes of private VLAN. Cannot configure PVLAN port mode on routed port.
A port with PVLAN port mode secondary/promiscuous cannot trunk all vlans.

x-enumDescriptions.ISL: Extends the PVLAN domain across multiple switches.
x-enumDescriptions.PROMISCUOUS: Configure the port to be a member of the primary VLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.SECONDARY: Set the port as a member of a secondary VLAN.

routing.description: Enable routing on the interface to make it layer-3 capable.

speed-downshift-enable.description:
Enable speed downshift for 1000BASE-T port. This is a non-standard mode for use only when standards-based
auto-negotiation is not able to establish a stable link. Enabling this may cause the port to link at a
lower than expected speed and should not be used on ports that are members of a LAG.

speed-duplex.description: Set ethernet speed and duplex based on port type and inserted transceiver.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Auto Negotiation for speed and duplex mode.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO_10MB_100MB: 10 Mb/sec or 100 Mb/sec and auto-negotiate link parameters.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO_1GB_2500MB: 1 Gb/sec or 2500 Mb/sec and auto-negotiate link parameters.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO_1GB_2500MB_5GB: 1 Gb/sec or 2500 Mb/sec or 5 Gb/sec and auto-negotiate link parameters.
x-enumDescriptions.AUTO_2500MB_5GB: 2500 Mb/sec or 5 Gb/sec and auto-negotiate link parameters.

speed-override.description: Override default transceiver speed or auto-negotiation.

items.description: Classifying traffic as trusted based on the VLAN.
items.x-typeDescription: Comma separated list of VLAN IDs and ranges

unknown-vlans.description:
Configure GVRP unknown VLANs handling mode for ethernet interface.
 Learn: Accept join requests for new VLANs and propagate through GVRP.
 Block: Process GVRP packets for known VLANs only, ignore new VLANs.
 Disable: Ignore all GVRP packets.

x-enumDescriptions.BLOCK:
Only process GVRP packets that concern themselves with
 known VLANs and ignore new VLANs.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Ignore all GVRP packets.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARN:
Accept join requests for new VLANs on this port and
 propagate requests through all other forwarding ports
 that are participating in GVRP.

unknown-vlans.x-typeDescription: Unknown VLANs handling mode for GVRP on port channel interfaces.

proxy-arp.description:
Enable proxy ARP.
Possible values:
CLIENT: Enables proxy ARP in CLIENT mode.
        Gateway responds to ARP requests with client's MAC address.
GATEWAY: Enables proxy ARP in GATEWAY mode.
         Gateway responds to ARP requests with its own MAC address.

is disabled globally by default. When globally enabled,
it is enabled on all interfaces by default. This leaf allows
disabling of DCBx per-interface.

x-enumDescriptions.TX_RX: Enable interface to both transmit and receive LLDP(Link Layer Discovery Protocol) packets.

items.description:
List of VLANs for which MVRP messages are ignored. The forbidden-vlan-list
config is supported only when registration is set to FORBIDDEN.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

join-timer.description: Specifies the timer interval to wait before sending MVRP protocol data units(PDUs).

leave-timer.description:
Specifies the timer interval before registrar is moved to EMPTY state from the
leave-all state. The leave timer must be at least double the join timer.

leaveall-timer.description:
Specifies the frequency of generated leave-all messages.
The leave timer must be less than the leaveall timer.

periodic-timer.description: Specifies the fequency of generated periodic messages.

periodic-timer-enable.description: Enables the Periodic Timer transmission for the port.

registration.description: Determines how interface responds to incoming MVRP messages.

x-enumDescriptions.FIXED:
Configure interface to ignore all MVRP(Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol)
messages and remain in registered state.
x-enumDescriptions.FORBIDDEN:
Configure interface to ignore all MVRP(Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol)
messages and remain in unregistered state.If forbidden-vlan-list
is not configured, then none of the VLANs are learned on this port. If
forbidden-vlan-list is configured , then those specific vlans
are not learned on this port.
x-enumDescriptions.NORMAL:
Configure interface to respond normally to incoming
MVRP(Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol) messages.

registration.x-typeDescription: MVRP Registration mode of the interface.

arp.description: Neighbor Discovery Manager parameters for ipv4.

nd.description: Neighbor Discovery Manager parameters for ipv6.

arp.description: Neighbor Discovery Manager parameters for ipv4.

nd.description: Neighbor Discovery Manager parameters for ipv6.

poe.description:
Power-over-ethernet(PoE) configurations.
The power-over-ethernet (PoE) manages the power supplied to
devices using standard Ethernet data cables.

allocation-method.description:
Power allocation method. By default, power allocation is automatic based on device usage.
Allowed configurable values are 'usage' or 'class' or 'value'

assigned-class.description:
Limit PoE power based on the assigned class.
Class 3 (13W max at powered device (PD))
Class 4 (25.5W max at powered device (PD))
Class 6 (51W max at  powered device (PD))
Class 8 (71.3W max at  powered device (PD))

cycle-timeout.description: Timeout value in units of seconds to control POE power delivery.

enabled.description:
Enable/Disable power-over-ethernet on the interface.
PoE on each port can be Enabled/Disabled individually.

lldp-detect.description:
Allow the link partner to specify power allocation using LLDP.
By default, LLDP power information is ignored.

pd-class-override.description:
The pd-class-override enables a device to draw power more than the
the power limit specified in IEEE classification.
By default,it is disabled. Power allocation method should be set
to 'class' when override needs to be enabled.

poe-cisco.description: Enable or disable PoE Cisco in the ethernet interface.

power-pairs.description:
Configure what cable pairs deliver power.
A 2-Pair power sourcing equipment (PSE) will source power on just one pairset, Alt-A or Alt-B.
A 4-Pair power sourcing equipment (PSE) will source power on both pairsets Alt-A and Alt-B

x-enumDescriptions.ALT_A: Delivers power only on the ALT-A pair.
x-enumDescriptions.ALT_A_AND_B: Delivers power on the ALT-A and ALT-B pairs.

power-pairs.x-typeDescription: Configure what cable pairs deliver power.

pre-std-detect.description:
Enable detection of pre-standard PoE devices. When 'pre-std-detect' is enabled,
power will be delivered on 'alt-a' pair only. By default 'pre-std-detect' is disabled.

priority.description:
Configure PoE priority in the event of power over-subscription.
Configurable values for priority on the interface are low, critical or high. Default priority is low

value.description:
Set the maximum power allocation for the port.
Applicable only when 'poe-allocate-by' is set to 'value'.

trunk-type.description:
Type of trunk.
 For gateways, the aggregation types supported are TRUNK for static and LACP for dynamic.
 The change of LAG type is not allowed without deleting the members in it. All the members need to be
 deleted before changing the LAG type

x-enumDescriptions.MULTI_CHASSIS_STATIC: Static multi-chassis LAG which does not use LACP
x-enumDescriptions.TRUNK: Manual static trunk that does not use LACP protocol.

unknown-vlans.description:
Configure GVRP unknown VLANs handling mode for port channel interface.
 Learn: Accept join requests for new VLANs and propagate through GVRP.
 Block: Process GVRP packets for known VLANs only, ignore new VLANs.
 Disable: Ignore all GVRP packets.

x-enumDescriptions.BLOCK:
Only process GVRP packets that concern themselves with
 known VLANs and ignore new VLANs.
x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Ignore all GVRP packets.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARN:
Accept join requests for new VLANs on this port and
 propagate requests through all other forwarding ports
 that are participating in GVRP.

unknown-vlans.x-typeDescription: Unknown VLANs handling mode for GVRP on port channel interfaces.

qinq.description:
QinQ interface level configuration.This feature
is not supported.

qinq-port-type.description:
QinQ port modes. The QinQ port type config allowed on trunk port only.
and is not allowed along with selective QinQ config.
The qinq-svlan parameter should be enabled on the native VLAN of the port

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Port as auto.
x-enumDescriptions.CUSTOMER_NETWORK:
Sets QinQ port type as customer-network port.
Customer-Network port is an access port facing user network,
that carries CVLANs traffic entering the port with QinQ tunnel using SVLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.PROVIDER_NETWORK:
Sets Qinq port type as a provider network type.
Provider-Network port is a trunk port facing provider network,
that transmits and receives service tag frames for multiple users.

items.description:
Customer VLANs. Service VLAN is mandatory along with customer VLANs.
Selective QinQ config allowed on access port only.
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

service-vlan.description:
Service VLAN to which the customer vlans are to be mapped.
Customer VLANs is mandatory along with service VLAN.
The qinq-svlan parameter should be enabled on the service-vlan
service-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ArubaInterfaceCommon_SpanningTreeConfig.description: Top-level container for spanning tree configuration.

stp.description: Interface level spanning-tree configuration. This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

admin-edge-port.description:
Configures the port as a spanning tree administrative edge port for the role.
This configuration removes the port participation from STP interactions when
onboarding devices. This in turn helps in faster onboarding of devices.
In GW this is applicable for access ports

admin-edge-port-trunk.description: Configures the TRUNK port as a spanning tree administrative edge port for the role.

bpdu-filter.description:
Stop transmitting BPDUs (Bridge Protocol Data Unit), receiving BPDUs,
and assume a continuous forwarding state.

bpdu-guard.description: Disable the port if the port receives STP BPDUs.

cost.description: Cost of the port for the instance.

loop-guard.description:
Guard against the loop and subsequently prevent it from becoming a
forwarding port.

mstp.description: STP parameters applicable per MSTP(Multiple Spanning Tree Protocol) instance.

native-vlan-ieee-bpdu.description:
Spanning tree native-vlan-ieee-bpdu config.This allows the device to send IEEE BPDUs
on the native VLAN when operating in the PVST mode.

priority.description: Port priority. For CX and PVOS this value will be multiplied by 16.

pvst-filter.description: Stop receiving and retransmitting PVST (Per-VLAN Spanning Tree) BPDUs.

pvst-guard.description: Disable the port if the port receives PVST BPDUs.

root-guard.description: Ignore superior BPDUs to prevent it from becoming root port.

rpvst.description: Cost and priority for a PVST VLAN.

vlan-id.description: VLAN to set cost and priority in PVST(Per VLAN Spanning Tree)
vlan-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

rpvst-filter.description: Stop receiving and retransmitting RPVST (Rapid Per-VLAN Spanning Tree) BPDUs.

rpvst-guard.description: Disable the port if the port receives RPVST BPDUs.

tcn-guard.description:
Stop propagating received topology changes notifications and topology
changes.

switchport.description: Switchport configuration. This configuration is valid only on a layer2 interface.

access-vlan.description: Assign the access VLAN to the access port.
access-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

interface-mode.description:
Interface access or trunk mode for VLANs.
The access-vlan should be set to null for configuring trunk mode and
native-vlan, trunk-vlan-ranges and trunk-vlan-all should be set to null while configuring access mode
Eg: switchport: {interface-mode: ACCESS, access-vlan: 600, native-vlan: null,
trunk-vlan-all: null, trunk-vlan-ranges: null}
switchport: {interface-mode: TRUNK, access-vlan: null, native-vlan: NATIVE_VLAN,
trunk-vlan-all: false, trunk-vlan-ranges: [ 1000 ]}

native-vlan.description:
Native VLAN id for untagged frames arriving on
a trunk interface.  Tagged frames sent on an interface
configured with a native VLAN should have their tags
stripped prior to transmission. This configuration is only
valid on a trunk interface.
native-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

tag.description:
This configuration is only valid on a trunk interface and the configured interface
does not accept untagged frames coming in and configured to be tagged on all VLANs.

trunk-vlan-all.description:
When interface mode is trunk allow all VLANs for trunk mode interface.
On CX devices, includes new VLANs as they are configured.

items.description:
VLAN ranges that the interface may carry when in trunk mode.
On CX devices, only applies currently configured VLANs.
items.x-typeDescription:
VLANs or only single-tagged VLANs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid VLAN IDs
(1 <= vlan-id <= 4094). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any VLAN-ID matching x <= VLAN-ID <= y falls within
the range.

This configured value will take the highest precedence in the selection logic
the switch uses to identify the IPv4 address to be advertised in the management address TLV.

ArubaInterfaceCommon_UrpfCheckConfig.description: Grouping for Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) configuration.

urpf.description: Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) on the interface to prevent IP address spoofing.

ipv4-enable.description:
Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) mode on the interface to prevent IPv4 address spoofing by validating
the source address of incoming packets.

x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Disable source address validation on incoming packets.
x-enumDescriptions.LOOSE: Incoming packets are accepted as long as the source IP address is reachable via any interface on the router.
x-enumDescriptions.STRICT:
Incoming packets are accepted only if they are received on the interface that the router would use to
reach the source IP address.

ipv4-enable.x-typeDescription: Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding mode for incoming packets on the interface.

ArubaInterfaceCommon_UrpfCheckConfigGet.description: Grouping for Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) configuration.

urpf.description: Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) on the interface to prevent IP address spoofing.

Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) mode on the interface to prevent IPv4 address spoofing by validating
the source address of incoming packets.

x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Disable source address validation on incoming packets.
x-enumDescriptions.LOOSE: Incoming packets are accepted as long as the source IP address is reachable via any interface on the router.
x-enumDescriptions.STRICT:
Incoming packets are accepted only if they are received on the interface that the router would use to
reach the source IP address.

ipv4-enable.x-typeDescription: Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding mode for incoming packets on the interface.

origin.description:
A port with a VLAN translation configuration allows traffic only for the translated VLAN and the native VLAN;
if it is a member of more VLANS, it does not allow traffic for them.
A translated VLAN must be present on the switch before the rule is created; the original VLAN need not be present.
VLAN translation and MVRP cannot be enabled on the same interface.
origin.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

translated.description: Translated VLAN identifier and not allowed on native VLAN 1.
translated.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

origin.description:
A port with a VLAN translation configuration allows traffic only for the translated VLAN and the native VLAN;
if it is a member of more VLANS, it does not allow traffic for them.
A translated VLAN must be present on the switch before the rule is created; the original VLAN need not be present.
VLAN translation and MVRP cannot be enabled on the same interface.
origin.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

translated.description: Translated VLAN identifier and not allowed on native VLAN 1.
translated.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

shutdown-on-split.description:
Determines whether the port will be shutdown when VSX split occurs.
This is only applicable to the ports that are not configured as MCLAG
and SVIs that do not have any MCLAGs as members.

Determines whether the port will be shutdown when VSX split occurs.
This is only applicable to the ports that are not configured as MCLAG
and SVIs that do not have any MCLAGs as members.

interface.description: Represents a single physical Ethernet or USB interface configuration on the device. Each interface is uniquely identified by its name (e.g., 0/0/1, enet0, usb0). Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete an interface configuration instance.

alternate-linkup.description: Enables Direct Attach Copper (DAC) alternate link detection mechanism for improved cable compatibility. This feature helps resolve link-up issues with certain DAC cables by using an alternative link establishment method. Useful for troubleshooting connectivity problems with passive copper cables. Corresponds to the dac-alternate-linkup command on gateway devices.

ap-profile.description: AP Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

br-profile.description: BRIDGE Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

description.description:
Description of the interface.
AOS-CX: length: 1-64

duplex.description: Configures the duplex mode for the Ethernet interface. Full-duplex allows simultaneous bidirectional communication for maximum throughput. Half-duplex only permits one direction at a time and is rarely used in modern networks. Auto-negotiation (default) automatically selects the best duplex mode supported by both devices. Manual settings must match on both ends of the link to avoid connectivity issues.

items.description: Forbid vlans for interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

gw-profile.description: References an existing Gateway Port Profile to apply centrally managed port policies to this interface. Gateway profiles provide template-based configuration for firewall rules, AAA settings, and other gateway-specific features. When assigned, profile settings override local interface configurations unless the retain-profile-configs option is enabled.

items.description: Isolated VLAN ID for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

jumbo-mtu.description: Enables support for Jumbo frames (Ethernet frames larger than the standard 1500-byte MTU, typically up to 9000 bytes). Jumbo frames improve network throughput and reduce CPU overhead for large data transfers such as storage traffic, backups, and virtualization workloads. All devices in the network path must support Jumbo frames to prevent fragmentation. Corresponds to the jumbo command on gateway devices.

name.description: Unique identifier for the Ethernet or USB interface. Supported formats include slot-based naming (e.g., 0/0/1 for slot 0, module 0, port 1) and logical naming (e.g., enet0, usb0). Device models enforce specific port ranges through capability constraints (e.g., gateway model GW_7005 supports 0/0/[0-3], GW_7205 supports 0/0/[0-5]). This field is mandatory and serves as the primary key for the interface instance.

port-monitor.description: Configures this interface as a monitoring port that receives mirrored traffic from another source interface (SPAN/port mirroring). The source can be a physical GigabitEthernet interface or a Port-Channel/LAG. All traffic from the source interface is duplicated and sent to this monitoring port for analysis, troubleshooting, or security monitoring purposes. Gateway devices only.

enable.description: Enables or disables port monitoring (SPAN/traffic mirroring) on this interface. When enabled, this interface receives a copy of all traffic from the specified source interface for analysis. Operational constraints: Only one monitoring session can be active on the device at a time. LLDP must be disabled on the monitoring port (enforced by system). Neither the monitoring port nor the monitored source port can be part of a Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) group or port-channel. The source interface must be different from the monitoring interface. Use this for network troubleshooting, traffic analysis, or security monitoring.

src-interface-id.description: Identifies the source interface to be monitored. For physical ports, use slot/module/port format (e.g., 0/0/1). For port-channels, use the LAG number (e.g., 5). This must be different from the monitoring interface identifier to prevent loops. The system enforces that source and destination interfaces cannot be the same.

src-interface-type.description: Specifies the type of source interface whose traffic will be mirrored to this monitoring port. GIGABIT_ETHERNET mirrors traffic from a single physical Ethernet port. PORT_CHANNEL mirrors aggregated traffic from all member ports in a Link Aggregation Group (LAG).

x-enumDescriptions.GIGABIT_ETHERNET: Source interface is a physical GigabitEthernet port. Traffic from this port will be mirrored to the monitoring interface.
x-enumDescriptions.PORT_CHANNEL: Source interface is a Link Aggregation Group (LAG/Port-Channel). Aggregated traffic from all member ports will be mirrored.

src-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the type of network interface that will be monitored (source) for traffic mirroring purposes. Gateway devices only.

port-type.description: Designates the interface as either a WAN (Wide Area Network) or LAN (Local Area Network) port. WAN ports typically connect to external networks or service providers and may apply different security policies, NAT rules, and routing behaviors. LAN ports connect to internal trusted networks. This designation affects firewall rules, routing decisions, and security policy enforcement.
port-type.x-typeDescription: For microbranch AP, port type WAN means uplink, port type LAN means downlink

portchannel-lag.description: Attach a link aggreagatioin group (LAG) to physical interface.

retain-profile-configs.description:
To decide whether or not to retain profile configs on an interface when
the profile is removed from the said interface

speed.description: Configures the link speed for the Ethernet interface. When set to SPEED_AUTO (default), the interface automatically negotiates the highest supported speed with the connected device. Manual speed settings force the interface to operate at a specific rate, which may require matching configuration on the peer device.

speed-mode.description: Configures the operational speed mode for multi-rate capable interfaces. Only supported on 7280 hardware. Determines whether the interface operates in 40Gbps, 10Gbps, or 1Gbps mode. Changing this setting may affect port availability or require a reboot. Consult hardware documentation for supported speed modes per interface.

sw-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

trusted.description: Marks the interface as trusted for security policy purposes. Trusted interfaces bypass certain security checks and firewall rules, allowing unrestricted communication. Typically applied to internal management interfaces or connections to known secure networks. Use with caution as this reduces security enforcement on the interface.

tunneled-node-port.description: Enables tunneled node functionality on the interface, allowing it to participate in overlay network tunneling. When enabled, traffic received on this port can be automatically encapsulated and forwarded through SD-WAN or VPN tunnels based on routing policies. Typically used for branch office deployments where local devices need transparent access to central resources over secure tunnels.

ArubaInterfaceEthernet_EthernetInterface.description: Ethernet interfaces provide physical connectivity for network devices including switches, gateways, access points, and bridges. This module supports configuration of interface properties, port profiles, link aggregation, Power over Ethernet (PoE), security features (AAA, port-security, LLDP), Layer 3 routing capabilities, and advanced features like PIM multicast, flow telemetry, and port monitoring. Supported scopes: Device. This configuration relies on Port Profile configurations (sw-profile for switches, gw-profile for gateways, ap-profile for access points, br-profile for bridges) for template-based settings. Interface names follow device-specific patterns (e.g., gateway model GW_7005 supports 0/0/[0-3], GW_7205 supports 0/0/[0-5]). Use this API to retrieve the list of Ethernet Interface configurations.

alternate-linkup.description: Enables Direct Attach Copper (DAC) alternate link detection mechanism for improved cable compatibility. This feature helps resolve link-up issues with certain DAC cables by using an alternative link establishment method. Useful for troubleshooting connectivity problems with passive copper cables. Corresponds to the dac-alternate-linkup command on gateway devices.

ap-profile.description: AP Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

br-profile.description: BRIDGE Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

description.description:
Description of the interface.
AOS-CX: length: 1-64
