Manual settings must match on both ends of the link to avoid connectivity issues.

src-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the type of network interface that will be monitored (source) for traffic mirroring purposes. Gateway devices only.

port-type.description: Designates the interface as either a WAN (Wide Area Network) or LAN (Local Area Network) port. WAN ports typically connect to external networks or service providers and may apply different security policies, NAT rules, and routing behaviors. LAN ports connect to internal trusted networks. This designation affects firewall rules, routing decisions, and security policy enforcement.
port-type.x-typeDescription: For microbranch AP, port type WAN means uplink, port type LAN means downlink

portchannel-lag.description: Attach a link aggreagatioin group (LAG) to physical interface.

retain-profile-configs.description:
To decide whether or not to retain profile configs on an interface when
the profile is removed from the said interface

speed.description: Configures the link speed for the Ethernet interface. When set to SPEED_AUTO (default), the interface automatically negotiates the highest supported speed with the connected device. Manual speed settings force the interface to operate at a specific rate, which may require matching configuration on the peer device.

speed-mode.description: Configures the operational speed mode for multi-rate capable interfaces. Only supported on 7280 hardware. Determines whether the interface operates in 40Gbps, 10Gbps, or 1Gbps mode. Changing this setting may affect port availability or require a reboot. Consult hardware documentation for supported speed modes per interface.

sw-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

trusted.description: Marks the interface as trusted for security policy purposes. Trusted interfaces bypass certain security checks and firewall rules, allowing unrestricted communication. Typically applied to internal management interfaces or connections to known secure networks. Use with caution as this reduces security enforcement on the interface.

tunneled-node-port.description: Enables tunneled node functionality on the interface, allowing it to participate in overlay network tunneling. When enabled, traffic received on this port can be automatically encapsulated and forwarded through SD-WAN or VPN tunnels based on routing policies. Typically used for branch office deployments where local devices need transparent access to central resources over secure tunnels.

alternate-linkup.description: Enables Direct Attach Copper (DAC) alternate link detection mechanism for improved cable compatibility. This feature helps resolve link-up issues with certain DAC cables by using an alternative link establishment method. Useful for troubleshooting connectivity problems with passive copper cables. Corresponds to the dac-alternate-linkup command on gateway devices.

ap-profile.description: AP Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

br-profile.description: BRIDGE Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

description.description:
Description of the interface.
AOS-CX: length: 1-64

duplex.description: Configures the duplex mode for the Ethernet interface. Full-duplex allows simultaneous bidirectional communication for maximum throughput. Half-duplex only permits one direction at a time and is rarely used in modern networks. Auto-negotiation (default) automatically selects the best duplex mode supported by both devices. Manual settings must match on both ends of the link to avoid connectivity issues.

items.description: Forbid vlans for interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

gw-profile.description: References an existing Gateway Port Profile to apply centrally managed port policies to this interface. Gateway profiles provide template-based configuration for firewall rules, AAA settings, and other gateway-specific features. When assigned, profile settings override local interface configurations unless the retain-profile-configs option is enabled.

items.description: Isolated VLAN ID for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

jumbo-mtu.description: Enables support for Jumbo frames (Ethernet frames larger than the standard 1500-byte MTU, typically up to 9000 bytes). Jumbo frames improve network throughput and reduce CPU overhead for large data transfers such as storage traffic, backups, and virtualization workloads. All devices in the network path must support Jumbo frames to prevent fragmentation. Corresponds to the jumbo command on gateway devices.

name.description: Unique identifier for the Ethernet or USB interface. Supported formats include slot-based naming (e.g., 0/0/1 for slot 0, module 0, port 1) and logical naming (e.g., enet0, usb0). Device models enforce specific port ranges through capability constraints (e.g., gateway model GW_7005 supports 0/0/[0-3], GW_7205 supports 0/0/[0-5]). This field is mandatory and serves as the primary key for the interface instance.

port-monitor.description: Configures this interface as a monitoring port that receives mirrored traffic from another source interface (SPAN/port mirroring). The source can be a physical GigabitEthernet interface or a Port-Channel/LAG. All traffic from the source interface is duplicated and sent to this monitoring port for analysis, troubleshooting, or security monitoring purposes. Gateway devices only.

enable.description: Enables or disables port monitoring (SPAN/traffic mirroring) on this interface. When enabled, this interface receives a copy of all traffic from the specified source interface for analysis. Operational constraints: Only one monitoring session can be active on the device at a time. LLDP must be disabled on the monitoring port (enforced by system). Neither the monitoring port nor the monitored source port can be part of a Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) group or port-channel. The source interface must be different from the monitoring interface. Use this for network troubleshooting, traffic analysis, or security monitoring.

src-interface-id.description: Identifies the source interface to be monitored. For physical ports, use slot/module/port format (e.g., 0/0/1). For port-channels, use the LAG number (e.g., 5). This must be different from the monitoring interface identifier to prevent loops. The system enforces that source and destination interfaces cannot be the same.

src-interface-type.description: Specifies the type of source interface whose traffic will be mirrored to this monitoring port. GIGABIT_ETHERNET mirrors traffic from a single physical Ethernet port. PORT_CHANNEL mirrors aggregated traffic from all member ports in a Link Aggregation Group (LAG).

x-enumDescriptions.GIGABIT_ETHERNET: Source interface is a physical GigabitEthernet port. Traffic from this port will be mirrored to the monitoring interface.
x-enumDescriptions.PORT_CHANNEL: Source interface is a Link Aggregation Group (LAG/Port-Channel). Aggregated traffic from all member ports will be mirrored.

src-interface-type.x-typeDescription: Defines the type of network interface that will be monitored (source) for traffic mirroring purposes. Gateway devices only.

port-type.description: Designates the interface as either a WAN (Wide Area Network) or LAN (Local Area Network) port. WAN ports typically connect to external networks or service providers and may apply different security policies, NAT rules, and routing behaviors. LAN ports connect to internal trusted networks. This designation affects firewall rules, routing decisions, and security policy enforcement.
port-type.x-typeDescription: For microbranch AP, port type WAN means uplink, port type LAN means downlink

portchannel-lag.description: Attach a link aggreagatioin group (LAG) to physical interface.

retain-profile-configs.description:
To decide whether or not to retain profile configs on an interface when
the profile is removed from the said interface

speed.description: Configures the link speed for the Ethernet interface. When set to SPEED_AUTO (default), the interface automatically negotiates the highest supported speed with the connected device. Manual speed settings force the interface to operate at a specific rate, which may require matching configuration on the peer device.

speed-mode.description: Configures the operational speed mode for multi-rate capable interfaces. Only supported on 7280 hardware. Determines whether the interface operates in 40Gbps, 10Gbps, or 1Gbps mode. Changing this setting may affect port availability or require a reboot. Consult hardware documentation for supported speed modes per interface.

sw-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

trusted.description: Marks the interface as trusted for security policy purposes. Trusted interfaces bypass certain security checks and firewall rules, allowing unrestricted communication. Typically applied to internal management interfaces or connections to known secure networks. Use with caution as this reduces security enforcement on the interface.

tunneled-node-port.description: Enables tunneled node functionality on the interface, allowing it to participate in overlay network tunneling. When enabled, traffic received on this port can be automatically encapsulated and forwarded through SD-WAN or VPN tunnels based on routing policies. Typically used for branch office deployments where local devices need transparent access to central resources over secure tunnels.

split-port-count.description: Split into number of children ports.

split-port-speed.description: Split into speed for children ports.

split-port-count.description: Split into number of children ports.

split-port-speed.description: Split into speed for children ports.

ArubaInterfaceManagement_MgmtInterfacesS.description:
Configuration for the Management Interface.
The device is configured or monitored through the
Management interface on mgmt VRF. All management
traffic such as device ssh, snmp, tftp and so on,
goes through the Management interface.

profile.description: Global profile for Management interface configurations

ArubaInterfacePortchannel_AaaLagConfig.description: Top-level grouping for interface AAA configuration.

ArubaInterfacePortchannel_AaaLagConfigGe.description: Top-level grouping for interface AAA configuration.

ArubaInterfacePortchannel_PortchannelCon.description: Grouping for the Port Channel (Aggregate) Interfaces.

interface.description:
Port channel interface aggregating multiple physical Ethernet ports
into a single logical trunk. Each entity is identified by portchannel
ID. Configure member ports, LACP parameters, VLANs, IP addresses,
routing protocols, and port-security settings. Device Limits: Gateway
supports port channels 0-7. Use these APIs to create, modify, or
delete a port channel interface.

items.description: Forbid vlans for interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: Isolated VLAN ID for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: Define the events that the SNMP Trap will monitor

x-enumDescriptions.AGED: On the enabled port a MAC is aged out.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARNED: On this port a MAC learn occurs.
x-enumDescriptions.MOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is moved.
x-enumDescriptions.REMOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is removed.

items.x-typeDescription: SNMP trap events

macsec-policy.description: MACsec policy to use on the interface.

mka-policy.description: MKA policy to use on the interface.

mvrp.description:
Interface level MVRP(Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol) configuration.
This is only supported when interface is in TRUNK mode.

items.description:
List of physical ports attached to the port channel interface. All the ports in this list should
have the same link-speed. If the link is not established while creating a portchannel, then choose
a link partner that has same speed.

pvlan-port-mode.description:
Port modes of private VLAN. Port with PVLAN(Private VLAN) port mode set to secondary or promiscuous
cannot trunk all VLANs. This config is not allowed on routed port.

x-enumDescriptions.ISL: Extends the PVLAN domain across multiple switches.
x-enumDescriptions.PROMISCUOUS: Configure the port to be a member of the primary VLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.SECONDARY: Set the port as a member of a secondary VLAN.

retain-port-profile-configs.description:
Controls whether profile configurations remain on the portchannel after
the profile is removed. When true, profile settings are preserved.
When false, the portchannel is deleted and the associated interfaces reset
to defaults. This leaf is automatically removed when a port profile is applied.

routing.description: Enable routing on the interface to make it layer-3 capable.

sw-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the portchannel.

sw-profile-alias.description: Alias reference for port profile for this portchannel

items.description: List of trusted VLANs.
items.x-typeDescription: Comma separated list of VLAN IDs and ranges

vrf-forwarding.description:
Add the interface to the VRF instance uniquely identified by its name.
This configuration is valid only on a layer3 interface.
vrf-forwarding.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

vrrp-profile-apply.description: Apply VRRP-interface profile on an interface.

ArubaInterfacePortchannel_PortchannelsSc.description:
Port Channel (Link Aggregation Group/LAG) interfaces combine multiple
physical Ethernet links into a single logical interface for increased
bandwidth, load balancing, and redundancy per IEEE 802.3ad. Port
channels support static aggregation and dynamic LACP negotiation with
features including VLAN trunking, routing protocols, access control,
and QoS policies. Configure port channel member interfaces, LACP
parameters, VLAN assignments, IP addressing, and link protection
settings. Supported scopes: Device. Device Limits: Gateway supports
port channels 0-7 (8 total). Use this API to retrieve the list of
port channel interfaces.

items.description: Forbid vlans for interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: Isolated VLAN ID for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: Define the events that the SNMP Trap will monitor

x-enumDescriptions.AGED: On the enabled port a MAC is aged out.
x-enumDescriptions.LEARNED: On this port a MAC learn occurs.
x-enumDescriptions.MOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is moved.
x-enumDescriptions.REMOVED: On the enabled port a MAC is removed.

items.x-typeDescription: SNMP trap events

macsec-policy.description: MACsec policy to use on the interface.

mka-policy.description: MKA policy to use on the interface.

mvrp.description:
Interface level MVRP(Multiple VLAN Registration Protocol) configuration.
This is only supported when interface is in TRUNK mode.

items.description:
List of physical ports attached to the port channel interface. All the ports in this list should
have the same link-speed. If the link is not established while creating a portchannel, then choose
a link partner that has same speed.

pvlan-port-mode.description:
Port modes of private VLAN. Port with PVLAN(Private VLAN) port mode set to secondary or promiscuous
cannot trunk all VLANs. This config is not allowed on routed port.

x-enumDescriptions.ISL: Extends the PVLAN domain across multiple switches.
x-enumDescriptions.PROMISCUOUS: Configure the port to be a member of the primary VLAN.
x-enumDescriptions.SECONDARY: Set the port as a member of a secondary VLAN.

retain-port-profile-configs.description:
Controls whether profile configurations remain on the portchannel after
the profile is removed. When true, profile settings are preserved.
When false, the portchannel is deleted and the associated interfaces reset
to defaults. This leaf is automatically removed when a port profile is applied.

routing.description: Enable routing on the interface to make it layer-3 capable.

sw-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the portchannel.

sw-profile-alias.description: Alias reference for port profile for this portchannel

items.description: List of trusted VLANs.
items.x-typeDescription: Comma separated list of VLAN IDs and ranges

vrf-forwarding.description:
Add the interface to the VRF instance uniquely identified by its name.
This configuration is valid only on a layer3 interface.
vrf-forwarding.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

vrrp-profile-apply.description: Apply VRRP-interface profile on an interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ApProfileConfigsGr.description:
AP uplink-port-profile and downlink-port-profile. This grouping establishes
the relationship their port configurations, allowing for differentiated
treatment of downlink and uplink ports. It enables the application of specific profiles
to different port types based on their role in the network topology.

ap-downlink-port-map.description:
List of all the downlink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's descretion is opted to fill the downlink port range. No validation
is performed to verify whether given port range is downlink ports

port-profile.description: AP Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

uplink-port-profile.description: AP uplink Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ApProfileConfigsGr.description:
AP uplink-port-profile and downlink-port-profile. This grouping establishes
the relationship their port configurations, allowing for differentiated
treatment of downlink and uplink ports. It enables the application of specific profiles
to different port types based on their role in the network topology.

ap-downlink-port-map.description:
List of all the downlink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's descretion is opted to fill the downlink port range. No validation
is performed to verify whether given port range is downlink ports

port-profile.description: AP Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

uplink-port-profile.description: AP uplink Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwLinkGrouping.description:
Gateway models and port-profile. This grouping establishes the relationship between
gateway hardware models and their port configurations. It enables the application of specific profiles
to different port types based on their role in the network topology.

gw-downlink-port-map.description:
List of all the downlink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's discretion is required to fill the downlink port range.
No validation is performed to verify whether given port range is downlink ports

downlink-port.description: Comma separated downlink Ports/range in format 0/0/1,0/0/2-0/0/4.
downlink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of hierarchical PORT IDs and ranges.
Each port is represented as X/Y/Z, where X, Y, and Z are integers,
and Z is in the range 0 to 64 (inclusive). A range is specified as
X/Y/Z1-X/Y/Z2, where Z1 and Z2 are valid port numbers (0 <= Z <= 64).
The range is assumed to be inclusive, such that any port matching
Z1 <= Z <= Z2 falls within the range. Multiple ports or ranges can be
specified, separated by commas. Example: 0/0/0,0/0/1-0/0/3,0/0/4

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwLinkGroupingGet.description:
Gateway models and port-profile. This grouping establishes the relationship between
gateway hardware models and their port configurations. It enables the application of specific profiles
to different port types based on their role in the network topology.

gw-downlink-port-map.description:
List of all the downlink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's discretion is required to fill the downlink port range.
No validation is performed to verify whether given port range is downlink ports

downlink-port.description: Comma separated downlink Ports/range in format 0/0/1,0/0/2-0/0/4.
downlink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of hierarchical PORT IDs and ranges.
Each port is represented as X/Y/Z, where X, Y, and Z are integers,
and Z is in the range 0 to 64 (inclusive). A range is specified as
X/Y/Z1-X/Y/Z2, where Z1 and Z2 are valid port numbers (0 <= Z <= 64).
The range is assumed to be inclusive, such that any port matching
Z1 <= Z <= Z2 falls within the range. Multiple ports or ranges can be
specified, separated by commas. Example: 0/0/0,0/0/1-0/0/3,0/0/4

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwModelGrouping.description:
A grouping for gateway model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to gateways with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

gw-model.description:
The model type of the gateway. This specifies the physical model identifier
for gateways across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts,and features like PoE support. This leaf is used to target Interface Profile
configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

x-enumDescriptions.AP_ALL: All types of models for AP
x-enumDescriptions.BRIDGE_ALL: All types of models for BRIDGE
x-enumDescriptions.GW_ALL: All types of models for AP
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_ALL: All types of models for SW_CX
x-enumDescriptions.SW_PVOS_ALL: All types of models for SW_PVOS

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwModelGroupingGet.description:
A grouping for gateway model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to gateways with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

gw-model.description:
The model type of the gateway. This specifies the physical model identifier
for gateways across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts,and features like PoE support. This leaf is used to target Interface Profile
configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

x-enumDescriptions.AP_ALL: All types of models for AP
x-enumDescriptions.BRIDGE_ALL: All types of models for BRIDGE
x-enumDescriptions.GW_ALL: All types of models for AP
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_ALL: All types of models for SW_CX
x-enumDescriptions.SW_PVOS_ALL: All types of models for SW_PVOS

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwProfileGrouping.description:
Gateway profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to gateway ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

gw-port-profile.description: Gateway Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_GwProfileGroupingG.description:
Gateway profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to gateway ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

gw-port-profile.description: Gateway Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_InterfaceProfilesS.description:
List of Interface Profile configuration. Interface Profile is bundle of interface configuration
for devices with specific sku/model. Defined for set of uplink/downlink ports for given models.
Interface Profile can be defined for stacks/chassis for fixed/automatic set of members/LineCards.
Interface Profile can be defined for custom stack/chassis size.
One Interface Profile can have port-profile config for uplink + downlink + custom ports for given
model/device type. In case of stacks, Interface Profile will not be applied VSF link ports, if they
are part of automatic/custom Interface Profile model+ports.

interface-profile.description:
Interface Profile configuration. It is a bundle of interface configuration
for devices with specific sku/model. Defined for set of uplink/downlink ports, or can
be for custom set of ports for given models. Interface Profile can be defined for stacks/chassis for
fixed/automatic set of members/LineCards. Interface Profile can be defined for custom stack/chassis size.
One Interface Profile can have port-profile config for uplink + downlink + custom ports for given
model/device type. In case of stacks, Interface Profile will not be applied VSF link ports, if they
are part of automatic/custom Interface Profile model+ports.

ap.description:
Interface Profile configuration for AP. This container enables
precise control over interface settings for each port.

description.description: Description for the Interface Profile config.

device-type.description:
Interface Profile device type. Device type can be for STANDALONE, STACK, MODULAR. With custom/automatic
specifications. This field cannot be edited once created.

x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_CHASSIS_AUTOMATIC: Interface Profile for AOSS Standalone chassis switches such as 5406R and 5412R for automatic workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_CHASSIS_CUSTOM: Interface Profile for AOSS VSF stack chassis switches made using 5406R and 5412R for custom workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_FLEXMODULE: Interface Profile for AOSS flexible modules for modular switchs.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_LINECARD: Interface Profile for AOSS linecards for chassis switchs.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_MODULAR_AUTOMATIC: Interface Profile for AOSS standalone modular switches such as 3810M and 2930M for automatic workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_MODULAR_CUSTOM: Interface Profile for AOSS standalone modular switches such as 3810M and 2930M for custom workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_STACK_AUTOMATIC: Interface Profile for AOSS Stack switches for automatic workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_STACK_CUSTOM: Interface Profile for AOSS Stack switches for custom workflow.
x-enumDescriptions.PVOS_SWITCH_STANDALONE: Interface Profile for AOSS Standalone switches such as 2530YA, 2530YB, 2540 and 2930F.
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_LINECARD: Line card interface profile used for Modular custom/automatic
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_MODULAR_AUTOMATIC: Includes profiles added to line-card module supported switches
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_MODULAR_CUSTOM: Includes custom interface profiles added to line-card module supported switches
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_STACK_AUTOMATIC: Includes stack profiles applied for all members
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_STACK_CUSTOM: Includes custom profiles applied to each stack member
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_VSX_MODULAR: Interface Profile for Modular VSX supported switches.
x-enumDescriptions.SWITCH_VSX_STANDALONE: Interface Profile for Standalone VSX supported switches.

device-type.x-typeDescription: Interface Profile applied to device types.

gateway-standalone.description:
Gateway port grouping. This container defines gateway configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for ports. It provides a
comprehensive framework for applying consistent port settings across gateway deployments with
specific hardware models.

pvos-switch-chassis-automatic.description:
Automatic Interface Profile configuration for chassis switches with modules. This container provides a
simplified configuration approach for chassis switches, applying consistent Interface Profile
settings across all installed line cards. It is ideal for homogeneous chassis
deployments where all modules serve similar functions in the network.

pvos-switch-chassis-custom.description:
Custom Interface Profile configuration for chassis switches with modules. This container enables
precise control over interface settings for each line card in a chassis switch.
It supports advanced network designs where different modules may require specialized
configurations based on their position, connectivity, or functional role.

pvos-switch-linecard.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines linecard switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

pvos-switch-standalone.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines standalone switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-linecard.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines linecard switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-modular-automatic.description:
Automatic Interface Profile configuration for switches with modules. This container provides a
simplified configuration approach for modular switches, applying consistent Interface Profile
settings across all installed line cards. It is ideal for homogeneous modular
deployments where all modules serve similar functions in the network.

switch-modular-custom.description:
Custom Interface Profile configuration for switches with modules. This container enables
precise control over interface settings for each line card in a modular switch.
It supports advanced network designs where different modules may require specialized
configurations based on their position, connectivity, or functional role.

switch-stack-automatic.description:
Switch stack automatic port grouping. This container enables uniform Interface Profile configuration
across all members in a switch stack with identical hardware. It simplifies management
by allowing administrators to define a single configuration that is automatically
applied to all stack members, ensuring consistent behavior across the stack.

switch-stack-custom.description:
Switch stack custom port grouping. This container supports heterogeneous stack
configurations by allowing different Interface Profiles for each member in the stack.
It provides granular control over interface settings for each stack member,
accommodating different hardware models or functional roles within the stack.

switch-standalone.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines standalone switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-vsx-modular.description:
VSX Modular Interface Profile configuration. This container enables configuration
of VSX pairs with chassis/modular switches, providing redundancy and high availability
with full line-card support. Both Peer1 and Peer2 must use matching VSX-supported
chassis models (5420, 6405, 6410, 8400). Exception: 6405 and 6410 models can be paired
together. Each peer can have independent line-card configurations while maintaining
compatible hardware models.

modular-peer1.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer1 modular/chassis switch. This peer represents the primary
device in a VSX pair configuration for chassis-based switches with line cards.
Supported VSX chassis models: 5420, 6405, 6410, 8400.

modular-peer2.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer2 modular/chassis switch. This peer represents the secondary
device in a VSX pair configuration. Both peers must have matching switch models, with
the exception that 6405 and 6410 models can be paired together.
Supported VSX chassis models: 5420, 6405, 6410, 8400.

switch-vsx-standalone.description:
VSX Standalone Interface Profile configuration. This container enables configuration
of VSX pairs with standalone switches, providing redundancy and high availability.
Both Peer1 and Peer2 must use matching VSX-supported standalone models (6300, 8100,
8320, 8325, 8360, 8400, 9300, 10000). Each peer can have independent port profiles
while maintaining consistent hardware models.

peer1.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer1 standalone switch. This peer represents the primary
device in a VSX pair configuration.

peer2.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer2 standalone switch. This peer represents the secondary
device in a VSX pair configuration. Both peers must have matching switch models.

ap.description:
Interface Profile configuration for AP. This container enables
precise control over interface settings for each port.

description.description: Description for the Interface Profile config.

device-type.description:
Interface Profile device type. Device type can be for STANDALONE, STACK, MODULAR. With custom/automatic
specifications. This field cannot be edited once created.

pvos-switch-linecard.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines linecard switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

pvos-switch-standalone.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines standalone switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-linecard.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines linecard switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-modular-automatic.description:
Automatic Interface Profile configuration for switches with modules. This container provides a
simplified configuration approach for modular switches, applying consistent Interface Profile
settings across all installed line cards. It is ideal for homogeneous modular
deployments where all modules serve similar functions in the network.

switch-modular-custom.description:
Custom Interface Profile configuration for switches with modules. This container enables
precise control over interface settings for each line card in a modular switch.
It supports advanced network designs where different modules may require specialized
configurations based on their position, connectivity, or functional role.

switch-stack-automatic.description:
Switch stack automatic port grouping. This container enables uniform Interface Profile configuration
across all members in a switch stack with identical hardware. It simplifies management
by allowing administrators to define a single configuration that is automatically
applied to all stack members, ensuring consistent behavior across the stack.

switch-stack-custom.description:
Switch stack custom port grouping. This container supports heterogeneous stack
configurations by allowing different Interface Profiles for each member in the stack.
It provides granular control over interface settings for each stack member,
accommodating different hardware models or functional roles within the stack.

switch-standalone.description:
Switch port grouping. This container defines standalone switch configurations,
including model specification, port types, and interface mappings for both uplink
and downlink ports. It provides a comprehensive framework for applying consistent
port settings across standalone switch deployments with specific hardware models.

switch-vsx-modular.description:
VSX Modular Interface Profile configuration. This container enables configuration
of VSX pairs with chassis/modular switches, providing redundancy and high availability
with full line-card support. Both Peer1 and Peer2 must use matching VSX-supported
chassis models (5420, 6405, 6410, 8400). Exception: 6405 and 6410 models can be paired
together. Each peer can have independent line-card configurations while maintaining
compatible hardware models.

modular-peer1.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer1 modular/chassis switch. This peer represents the primary
device in a VSX pair configuration for chassis-based switches with line cards.
Supported VSX chassis models: 5420, 6405, 6410, 8400.

modular-peer2.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer2 modular/chassis switch. This peer represents the secondary
device in a VSX pair configuration. Both peers must have matching switch models, with
the exception that 6405 and 6410 models can be paired together.
Supported VSX chassis models: 5420, 6405, 6410, 8400.

switch-vsx-standalone.description:
VSX Standalone Interface Profile configuration. This container enables configuration
of VSX pairs with standalone switches, providing redundancy and high availability.
Both Peer1 and Peer2 must use matching VSX-supported standalone models (6300, 8100,
8320, 8325, 8360, 8400, 9300, 10000). Each peer can have independent port profiles
while maintaining consistent hardware models.

peer1.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer1 standalone switch. This peer represents the primary
device in a VSX pair configuration.

peer2.description:
Configuration for VSX Peer2 standalone switch. This peer represents the secondary
device in a VSX pair configuration. Both peers must have matching switch models.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ModularAutomaticGr.description:
Modular automatic container with line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all
linecard in a modular chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Linecard Interface Profile references. Only LINECARD type interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of the Stack/Modular should match with
linecard Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ModularAutomaticGr.description:
Modular automatic container with line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all
linecard in a modular chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Linecard Interface Profile references. Only LINECARD type interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of the Stack/Modular should match with
linecard Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ModularCustomGroup.description:
Modular custom grouping. This grouping enables detailed configuration of modular switches
with line cards.

line-cards.description:
Detailed configuration of modular switches with line cards, allowing administrators to
specify different Interface Profiles for each module based on its port type. It supports heterogeneous chassis
deployments where line cards may have different port densities.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_ModularCustomGroup.description:
Modular custom grouping. This grouping enables detailed configuration of modular switches
with line cards.

line-cards.description:
Detailed configuration of modular switches with line cards, allowing administrators to
specify different Interface Profiles for each module based on its port type. It supports heterogeneous chassis
deployments where line cards may have different port densities.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosChassisAutomat.description:
Chassis automatic container with line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all
linecard in a chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Linecard Interface Profile references. Only PVOS_SWITCH_LINECARD type interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of thechassis should match with
linecard Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosChassisAutomat.description:
Chassis automatic container with line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all
linecard in a chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Linecard Interface Profile references. Only PVOS_SWITCH_LINECARD type interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of thechassis should match with
linecard Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosChassisCustomG.description:
Chassis custom grouping. This grouping enables detailed configuration of chassis switches
with line cards.

pvos-line-cards.description:
Detailed configuration of chassis switches with line cards, allowing administrators to
specify different Interface Profiles for each module based on its port type. It supports heterogeneous chassis
deployments where line cards may have different port densities.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosChassisCustomG.description:
Chassis custom grouping. This grouping enables detailed configuration of chassis switches
with line cards.

pvos-line-cards.description:
Detailed configuration of chassis switches with line cards, allowing administrators to
specify different Interface Profiles for each module based on its port type. It supports heterogeneous chassis
deployments where line cards may have different port densities.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchChassisM.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

pvos-switch-model.description:
The model type of the switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created. Allowed model is 5400R.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchChassisM.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

pvos-switch-model.description:
The model type of the switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created. Allowed model is 5400R.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchLinkGrou.description:
Switch models and port-profile. This grouping establishes the relationship between
switch hardware models and their port configurations, allowing for differentiated
treatment of uplink and downlink ports. It enables the application of specific profiles
to different port types based on their role in the network topology.

pvos-downlink-port-map.description:
List of all the downlink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's discretion is required to fill the downlink port range.
No validation is performed to verify whether given port range is uplink/downlink ports

pvos-downlink-port.description: Comma separated downlink Ports/range in format 1,2-4.
pvos-downlink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of PORT IDs and ranges.
PortID or only single-tagged PortIDs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid Port IDs
(1 <= port-id <= 64). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any Port-ID matching x <= Port-ID <= y falls within
the range. Example 1,2-3,4

pvos-uplink-port-map.description:
List of all the uplink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's discretion is opted to fill the uplink port range. No validation
is performed to verify whether given port range is uplink ports

pvos-uplink-port.description: Comma separated Uplink Ports/range in format 1,2-4.
pvos-uplink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of PORT IDs and ranges.
PortID or only single-tagged PortIDs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid Port IDs
(1 <= port-id <= 64). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any Port-ID matching x <= Port-ID <= y falls within
the range. Example 1,2-3,4

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchModelGro.description:
A grouping for AOSS switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

pvos-switch-model.description:
The model type of the AOSS switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchModelGro.description:
A grouping for AOSS switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

pvos-switch-model.description:
The model type of the AOSS switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchPortType.description:
A grouping for port-type. This grouping encapsulates the physical characteristics
and capabilities of switch ports, allowing Interface Profile configurations to target specific
port types based on their media, speed, and functional capabilities. It enables
precise control over port configurations across different hardware platforms.

pvos-port-type.description:
Port type specification including PoE capability, copper/fiber media type,
total port count, and other port-specific features for the switch model.
This defines which ports on the device will be included in the Interface Profile configuration,
such as all ports, only copper ports, only fiber ports, or specific subsets
of ports based on their capabilities and intended usage.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchProfileG.description:
Switch profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to switch ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

pvos-port-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_PvosSwitchProfileG.description:
Switch profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to switch ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

pvos-port-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_StackAutomaticGrou.description:
Stack/Modular automatic container with member/line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all members
of a stack or modules in a modular chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Standalone Interface Profile references. Only standalone interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of the Stack/Modular should match with
standalone Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_StackAutomaticGrou.description:
Stack/Modular automatic container with member/line-card info. This grouping provides
a framework for automatically applying consistent Interface Profile configurations across all members
of a stack or modules in a modular chassis. It simplifies administration by treating
multiple physical components as a single logical entity with uniform configuration.

items.description:
List of Standalone Interface Profile references. Only standalone interface-profile can
be present in the list. The model-type of the Stack/Modular should match with
standalone Interface Profile.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_StackCustomGroupin.description:
A grouping for defining custom stack configurations with detailed member information.
This grouping enables the creation of heterogeneous switch stacks with specific member counts,
varying port types, and individual Interface Profiles for each member. Unlike automatic stack
configurations that apply a uniform configuration across identical stack members, this
custom grouping allows for precise control over each stack member's configuration, supporting
deployments with mixed switch models or varying port requirements across the stack.

member-count.description:
The total number of switch members in the custom stack configuration. This value
determines how many switches will be managed as a single logical entity in the stack.
The range is limited to 0-10 members, where 0 indicates an empty stack definition
that can be populated later, and values 1-10 represent the actual number of physical
switches in the stack.

members.description:
A list containing configuration details for individual switch members in the stack.
Each entry represents a single physical switch with its own member ID, port configuration,
and Interface Profile references. This allows for heterogeneous stack configurations where
different switch models can be combined into a single managed stack. The list is limited
to a maximum of 10 elements, matching the maximum supported stack size.

member-id.description:
The unique identifier for a member switch within the stack. This ID serves as
the primary key for identifying individual switches in the stack configuration.
Valid values range from 0 to 10, corresponding to the physical position or
assigned ID of each switch in the stack topology.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_StackCustomGroupin.description:
A grouping for defining custom stack configurations with detailed member information.
This grouping enables the creation of heterogeneous switch stacks with specific member counts,
varying port types, and individual Interface Profiles for each member. Unlike automatic stack
configurations that apply a uniform configuration across identical stack members, this
custom grouping allows for precise control over each stack member's configuration, supporting
deployments with mixed switch models or varying port requirements across the stack.

member-count.description:
The total number of switch members in the custom stack configuration. This value
determines how many switches will be managed as a single logical entity in the stack.
The range is limited to 0-10 members, where 0 indicates an empty stack definition
that can be populated later, and values 1-10 represent the actual number of physical
switches in the stack.

members.description:
A list containing configuration details for individual switch members in the stack.
Each entry represents a single physical switch with its own member ID, port configuration,
and Interface Profile references. This allows for heterogeneous stack configurations where
different switch models can be combined into a single managed stack. The list is limited
to a maximum of 10 elements, matching the maximum supported stack size.

member-id.description:
The unique identifier for a member switch within the stack. This ID serves as
the primary key for identifying individual switches in the stack configuration.
Valid values range from 0 to 10, corresponding to the physical position or
assigned ID of each switch in the stack topology.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchChassisModel.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier

It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,

switch-model.description:

This field cannot be edited once created. Allowed model 6405, 6410, 8400, 8400X, 5420.
switch-model.x-typeDescription:
Chassis model types for modular switches. These models represent
chassis-based switch platforms that support line cards and modular

downlink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of PORT IDs and ranges.
PortID or only single-tagged PortIDs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid Port IDs
(1 <= port-id <= 64). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any Port-ID matching x <= Port-ID <= y falls within
the range. Example 1,2-3,4

uplink-port-map.description:
List of all the uplink ports and associated port-profiles references.
User's discretion is opted to fill the uplink port range. No validation
is performed to verify whether given port range is uplink ports

uplink-port.description: Comma separated Uplink Ports/range in format 1,2-4.
uplink-port.x-typeDescription:
Comma separated list of PORT IDs and ranges.
PortID or only single-tagged PortIDs or both. A range is
specified as x-y where x and y are valid Port IDs
(1 <= port-id <= 64). The range is assumed to be inclusive,
such that any Port-ID matching x <= Port-ID <= y falls within
the range. Example 1,2-3,4

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchModelGroupin.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

switch-model.description:
The model type of the switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchModelGroupin.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

switch-model.description:
The model type of the switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchPortTypeGrou.description:
A grouping for port-type. This grouping encapsulates the physical characteristics
and capabilities of switch ports, allowing Interface Profile configurations to target specific
port types based on their media, speed, and functional capabilities. It enables
precise control over port configurations across different hardware platforms.

port-type.description:
Port type specification including PoE capability, copper/fiber media type,
total port count, and other port-specific features for the switch model.
This defines which ports on the device will be included in the Interface Profile configuration,
such as all ports, only copper ports, only fiber ports, or specific subsets
of ports based on their capabilities and intended usage.
This field cannot be edited once created.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchProfileGroup.description:
Switch profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to switch ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

sw-port-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchProfileGroup.description:
Switch profile grouping. This grouping associates port profiles with interfaces,
providing a mechanism to apply predefined configuration templates to switch ports.
These profiles contain standardized settings for security, QoS, VLANs, and other
interface parameters that can be consistently applied across multiple devices.

sw-port-profile.description: Switch Port Profile (if any) associated with the interface.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchStackBaseMod.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

switch-series.description:
The model type of the switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for switches across different form factors and capabilities. Each model
type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port
counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support. This leaf
is used to target Interface Profile configurations to specific hardware models.
This field cannot be edited once created. Allowed series 6300, 6200, 6300L

x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6200: SW_CX 6200 is base model for both 6200M and 6200F member platforms
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6300: SW_CX 6300 is base model for both 6300M and 6300F member platforms
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6300L: SW_CX 6300L is base model for 6300L member platforms

switch-series.x-typeDescription:
Base model types for switch stacks. These models represent the core
switch platforms that support stack configurations with consistent
hardware capabilities across stack members.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_SwitchStackBaseMod.description:
A grouping for switch model. This grouping defines the hardware model identifier
used for associating specific Interface Profile configurations with compatible hardware platforms.
It enables targeting of configuration sets to switches with the same model type,
ensuring consistent interface settings across identical hardware.

switch-series.description:

type corresponds to a specific hardware platform with predetermined port

x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6200: SW_CX 6200 is base model for both 6200M and 6200F member platforms
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6300: SW_CX 6300 is base model for both 6300M and 6300F member platforms
x-enumDescriptions.SW_CX_6300L: SW_CX 6300L is base model for 6300L member platforms

switch-series.x-typeDescription:
Base model types for switch stacks. These models represent the core

ArubaInterfaceProfile_VsxPeerStandaloneG.description:
A grouping for VSX peer standalone switch configuration. This grouping defines the hardware
model identifier and port configuration for a VSX peer device, ensuring that both peers in
a VSX pair can be configured with consistent interface profiles.

switch-model.description:
The model type of the VSX peer switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for VSX-supported standalone switches. Each model type corresponds to a specific hardware
platform with predetermined port counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support.
Supported VSX standalone models: 6300, 8100, 8320, 8325, 8360, 8400, 9300, 10000.
This field cannot be edited once created.
switch-model.x-typeDescription:
Model types for VSX-supported standalone switches. These models can be used
in VSX configurations where two switches operate as a single logical entity
for high availability and redundancy. Supported models: 6300 (M/F/L), 8100,
8320, 8325 (including 8325P and 8325H), 8360, 9300 (including 9300S),
10000, 10040.

ArubaInterfaceProfile_VsxPeerStandaloneG.description:
A grouping for VSX peer standalone switch configuration. This grouping defines the hardware
model identifier and port configuration for a VSX peer device, ensuring that both peers in
a VSX pair can be configured with consistent interface profiles.

switch-model.description:
The model type of the VSX peer switch. This specifies the physical model identifier
for VSX-supported standalone switches. Each model type corresponds to a specific hardware
platform with predetermined port counts, uplink configurations, and features like PoE support.
Supported VSX standalone models: 6300, 8100, 8320, 8325, 8360, 8400, 9300, 10000.
This field cannot be edited once created.
switch-model.x-typeDescription:
Model types for VSX-supported standalone switches. These models can be used
in VSX configurations where two switches operate as a single logical entity
for high availability and redundancy. Supported models: 6300 (M/F/L), 8100,
8320, 8325 (including 8325P and 8325H), 8360, 9300 (including 9300S),
10000, 10040.

ArubaInterfaceQos_FlowControlConfig.description:
The interface's 802.3x or priority-based flow-control configuration. On
some platforms, memory pools must first be configured (using the
aruba-qos-pool yang model) before any interface flow-control configuration
will take effect.

cable-length.description:
Specifies the cable-length auto-detection mode or the value
 in meters used to compute the headroom limit for lossless traffic
 arriving on the interface. The default value is 350 meters.

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Automatically detect cable-length.

flow-control-mode.description:
Flow-control mode for the interface: Link Level Flow Control (LLFC),
Priority Flow Control (PFC), or none. On some platforms, memory pools
must first be configured (using the aruba-qos-pool yang model) before
any interface flow-control configuration will take effect.
flow-control-mode.x-typeDescription:
Specifies the type of flow control to apply - Link Level
Flow Control (LLFC), Priority Flow Control (PFC), or none.

llfc-flow-control.description:
Configuration for 802.3x link-level flow control (LLFC). On some
platforms, a memory pool must first be created (using the
aruba-qos-pool yang model) that matches the llfc-pool-id value before
any 802.3X link-level Tx or RxTx flow-control configuration will
take effect.

direction.description:
This mandatory field specifies whether the interface is enabled to
handle received flow-control frames, to transmit flow-control frames,
or both. This setting only applies to 802.3X link-level flow control.

x-enumDescriptions.RX: Respond to receiving a flow-control pause frame from the link peer.
x-enumDescriptions.RXTX:
Transmit bi-directional flow-control pause frames to the link peer and
respond to received flow-control pause frames from the link peer.
x-enumDescriptions.TX: Transmit flow-control pause frames to the link peer.

direction.x-typeDescription: Specifies the direction(s) in which flow control will be applied.

llfc-pool-id.description:
The pool ID for arriving lossless LLFC packets. On some platforms, a
memory pool with the same pool ID value must first be configured
(using the aruba-qos-pool yang model) before any 802.3X link-level Tx
or RxTx flow-control configuration will take effect.

override-negotiation.description:
Enables overriding link-negotiation results and using
the locally-specified flow-control mode instead.

lossless-buffer-based-link-level-flow-co.description: Buffer threshold configuration for 802.3x link-level flow control, including XOF and XON limits, headroom, and other parameters.

lossless-buffer-based-priority-flow-cont.description:
Buffer threshold configuration for 802.1Qbb priority flow control,
including XOFF and XON limits, headroom, and other parameters.

thresholds.description: Buffer-configuration threshold specific to each PFC priority.

priority.description: Packet priority value from the 802.1Q VLAN header.

pfc-watchdog.description:
Enables the priority flow control watchdog. The watchdog detects when
flow-controlled egress queues are paused for an excessive duration, and
takes action when the globally-configured trigger time has elapsed with
the queue remaining paused.

priority-config.description: Per-priority direction and buffer configuration for priority flow control.

direction.description:
This mandatory field specifies whether the interface priority is
enabled to handle received priority flow-control frames, transmit
priority flow-control frames, or both.

x-enumDescriptions.RX: Respond to receiving a flow-control pause frame from the link peer.
x-enumDescriptions.RXTX:
Transmit bi-directional flow-control pause frames to the link peer and
respond to received flow-control pause frames from the link peer.
x-enumDescriptions.TX: Transmit flow-control pause frames to the link peer.

direction.x-typeDescription: Specifies the direction(s) in which flow control will be applied.

priority.description:
Packet priority value found in the 802.1Q VLAN header. On some
platforms, one or more memory pools must first be created (using the
aruba-qos-pool yang model) and have the desired priorities assigned
to them before any Priority Flow Control (PFC) configuration will
take effect.

ArubaInterfaceQos_FlowControlConfigGet.description:
The interface's 802.3x or priority-based flow-control configuration. On
some platforms, memory pools must first be configured (using the
aruba-qos-pool yang model) before any interface flow-control configuration
will take effect.

priority.description:

platforms, one or more memory pools must first be created (using the
aruba-qos-pool yang model) and have the desired priorities assigned

headroom.description:
The maximum amount of headroom-buffer memory allowed to
 be consumed by in-flight traffic after the traffic has been PAUSED.

xoff-dynamic-threshold-alpha.description: The alpha value used in computing the limit when the XOFF limit-type is dynamic.

xoff-static-threshold.description: The fixed buffer-limit in kilobytes when the XOFF limit-type is static.

xon-delta.description:
The buffer utilization decrease-amount below the XOFF limit
 that will trigger a RESUME of traffic when it is PAUSED.

headroom.description:
The maximum amount of headroom-buffer memory allowed to
 be consumed by in-flight traffic after the traffic has been PAUSED.

xoff-dynamic-threshold-alpha.description: The alpha value used in computing the limit when the XOFF limit-type is dynamic.

xoff-static-threshold.description: The fixed buffer-limit in kilobytes when the XOFF limit-type is static.

xon-delta.description:
The buffer utilization decrease-amount below the XOFF limit
 that will trigger a RESUME of traffic when it is PAUSED.

qos.description: QoS interface parameters for the physical interface or port channel.
QoS interface parameters for the physical interface or port channel.

burst.description:
The burst size in units of kilobytes for the maximum egress traffic rate.
Valid when egress-rate is specified.

cos.description:
COS remark value for all packets arriving on the port. The new value
will be reflected in the outgoing VLAN tag.

AOSCX: Arriving packets will be re-marked upon transmit. The active
port trust mode must be 'None' before any cos remark value can be
configured.
PVOS: Arriving packets will be re-marked upon arriving, which will
affect queueing.

dscp.description:
The DSCP remark for the port. The new value will be reflected in any
outgoing IP packet headers.

AOSCX: Arriving packets will be re-marked upon transmit. The active
port trust mode must be 'None' before any dscp remark value can be
configured.
PVOS: Arriving packets will be re-marked upon arrival, which will
affect queueing.
dscp.x-typeDescription:
A differentiated services code point (DSCP) marking within the
IP header.

egress-rate.description:
The maximum egress traffic rate for the interface.
To configure this, the type of max-rate-units must be specified.

max-rate-units.description: Traffic rate units. Required when egress-rate is specified.

x-enumDescriptions.KBPS: Rate value is in kilobits per second.
x-enumDescriptions.MBPS: Rate value is in megabits per second.
x-enumDescriptions.PERCENT: Rate value is a percentage of link bandwidth.
x-enumDescriptions.PPS: Rate value is in packets per second.

qvalue.description: The minimum bandwidth percentage for queue.

priority-mapping-bandwidth-scheduler.description:
Priority-mapping bandwidth-scheduler interface profile. Profiles must
first be created using the aruba-gw-scheduler yang model.

schedule-profile.description:
QoS interface schedule profile. The schedule profile must
first be configured using the aruba-qos-schedule yang model, and must be
valid for the platform on which the port resides. In addition, the
schedule profile must match the configured number of queues and queue ID
values in the globally-applied queue profile.
schedule-profile.x-typeDescription:
Name or description of the entity. Must be 1-64 characters in length and can only
contain alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, and hyphens

threshold-profile.description:
QoS interface threshold profile. The threshold profile must
first be configured using the aruba-qos-threshold-profile yang model, and
must be supportable by the platform on which the port resides.
threshold-profile.x-typeDescription:
Name or description of the entity. Must be 1-64 characters in length and can only
contain alphanumeric characters, periods, underscores, and hyphens

trust.description: QoS Trust Mode Configuration for the port.

x-enumDescriptions.DEFAULT: Trust 802.1p priority and preserve DSCP or IP-ToS.
x-enumDescriptions.DEVICE_ARUBA_AP: Trust DSCP from Aruba Access-point devices.
x-enumDescriptions.DEVICE_NONE: Remove all trusted device configuration for port.
x-enumDescriptions.DOT1P: Trust 802.1p priority and preserve DSCP or IP-ToS.
x-enumDescriptions.IP_PRECEDENCE: Trust IP-Precedence and remark the 802.1p priority to match.
x-enumDescriptions.NONE: Do not trust any priority fields.

trust.x-typeDescription: The QoS Trust Mode configuration for the port.

burst.description:
The burst size in units of kilobytes for the maximum egress traffic rate.
Valid when egress-rate is specified.

cos.description:

interface.description:
Layer 3 VLAN interface (SVI) enabling IP routing for a VLAN segment.
Each entity is identified by VLAN ID. Configure IP addresses
(IPv4/IPv6), DHCP services, routing protocol parameters, VRRP, active
gateway, and access policies for inter-VLAN routing. Device Limits:
Gateway supports max 3 IPv6 addresses, 16 IPv4 helper addresses, and 3
IPv6 helper addresses per VLAN interface. Use these APIs to create,
modify, or delete a VLAN interface.

bcmc-optimization.description:
Broadcast multicast optimization (BCMC) to prevent flooding. BCMC will drop unwanted broadcast and multicast
frames on a VLAN.

deny-inter-user-bridging.description: Drop all L2 traffic between users on this vlan except addresses in the allowed address list. Gateway and DNS addresses are auto learnt. Disabled by default

id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ip-neighbor-flood-enable.description: Enable VLAN flooding when a link goes down.

is-valid.description:
Indicates whether the VLAN interface is considered valid on the device.
Setting it to false to override the inherited configuration will
delete the VLAN interface on the device.
The default value is true at the time of creating the interface.

jumbo.description: Jumbo VLAN allowing you to pass packets up to 9216 bytes in size.

ArubaInterfaceVlan_VlanInterfacesSchemaG.description:
Layer 3 VLAN (Switched Virtual Interface/SVI) configurations provide
IP routing functionality for VLANs, enabling inter-VLAN routing and
gateway services. VLAN interfaces support IPv4/IPv6 addressing, DHCP
relay, routing protocol participation (OSPF, BGP, RIP), VRRP for high
availability, and access control policies. Configure VLAN interface IP
addresses, DHCP servers/relay agents, multicast protocols, routing
parameters, and security policies. Device Limits: Gateway supports max
3 IPv6 addresses, 16 IPv4 helper addresses, and 3 IPv6 helper
addresses per VLAN interface. Use this API to retrieve the list of
VLAN interfaces.

ArubaLacp_LacpSchema.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.

system-priority.description: System priority used in LACP negotiation.

ArubaLacp_LacpSchemaGet.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.

system-priority.description: System priority used in LACP negotiation.

ArubaLldp_LldpSchemaGET.description:
The Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) is an industry-standard,
vendor-neutral method to allow networked devices to advertise capabilities,
discover and identify other LLDP enabled devices and gather information in a
LAN. LLDP is supported on physical interfaces and Out-Of-Band Management (OOBM)
interfaces and it is not supported on logical interfaces.

ArubaMirrorEndpoint_MirrorEndpointSchema.description:
Mirror-Endpoints terminate a remote mirror tunnel. They serve to control packet
decapsulation and forwarding to the mirror destinations.
Creating a Mirror-Endpoint is independent of the corresponding mirror session,
which means there is no ordering requirement for the creation of a mirror session
and a Mirror Endpoint.

description.description: Configuration to set the description of a Mirror-Endpoint profile.

endpoints.description:
List of configured mirror endpoints.
AOS-CX: Max: 32.

name.description: Configuration to set the name of a Mirror-Endpoint profile.

destination-ip.description:
Tunnel destination IP.
AOS-CX: Set to same destination IP as Mirror session tunnel.
destination-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

encap.description: Configured Tunnel Encapsulation Type. Default is gre.

source-ip.description:
Tunnel source IP.
AOS-CX: Set to same source IP as Mirror session tunnel.
source-ip.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

source-udp.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

vrf.description: Source tunnel Virtual Routing and Forwarding (VRF).
vrf.x-typeDescription: Type definition for 'name', should be alphanumeric. Special characters such as '\', '/' and space are not allowed.

destination-type.description:

ArubaMirror_MirrorSession.description:
Mirror Session Configuration.
AOS-CX: A mirror session consists of one or more mirror sources, mirror destination(s), a comment and
an enabled state.

comment.description: Configuration to set the Mirror session comment.

enable.description:
Enable mirror session.
AOS-CX: New Mirror Sessions are automatically in the disabled state.  To activate
mirroring of traffic set enable to 'true'.

session-id.description:
Configuration to set the Mirror Session number.
AOS-CX: Numeric Mirror Session ID from 1-4 per switch. Referenced by policy mirrors.
session-id.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a Mirror session

ArubaMirror_MirrorsSchemaGET.description:
Mirror Configuration. Mirroring is the ability of a switch to
transmit a copy of a packet out another system interface. This allows
network administrators to replicate all traffic arriving and/or leaving
selected system interfaces for collection/analysis purposes.

be kept by the router while it waits for a new link

each link LDP adjacency. This configuration is valid only on a layer3 interface.

Sampled Flow (sFlow) configuration.sFlow is an industry-standard sampling
technology used to sample application-level packet flows and gather interface
statistics from network devices.
