ArubaSwPortProfile_SwPortProfilesSchemaG.description:
Configure and manage port profiles.
A port profile is a template for configuring ethernet interface configurations.
It is a mechanism intended to ease the process of configuring multiple interfaces
with same set of configurations. Instead of manually configuring each interface one-by-one,
the port profile collects the common configuration settings and allows them to be applied on
the interface or a collection of interfaces.

aaa-profile.description: Name of the profile containing AAA configurations.

dfp-profile.description: Name of the profile containing Device Fingerprinting configurations.

items.description: Configure forbidden vlans for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

items.description: Configure isolated VLANs for the interface.
items.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

enable-timer.description:
Time in seconds to wait before re-enabling the trunk disabled by LACP
feature for minimum active links.

mad-passthrough.description: MAD (Multi-Active Detection) passthrough on LACP trunks.

min-active-links.description:
Minimum number of active member links in a LACP
trunk group for it to be operational.

mode.description: Active/Passive mode of LACP for this group

x-enumDescriptions.AUTO: Auto detect LACP PDU to determine if LACP passive should be enabled or NOT.

port-id.description: Port ID used in LACP negotiation.
port-id.x-typeDescription:
A 16-bit port number used by a transport protocol such as TCP
or UDP.

port-priority.description: Port priority used in LACP negotiation.

x-enumDescriptions.FAST: LACP heartbeats are requested at the rate of one per second.
x-enumDescriptions.SLOW: LACP heartbeats are requested at the rate of one per 30 seconds.

qos-lag.description: QoS interface parameters for the physical interface or port channel.

dcbx-disable.description:
Enable DCBx (Data Center Bridging Exchange), which
is disabled globally by default. When globally enabled,
it is enabled on all interfaces by default. This leaf allows
disabling of DCBx per-interface.

enable-notification.description: Set enable notification on the specified ports.

mode.description: Set the LLDP operational mode.

x-enumDescriptions.DISABLE: Disable both transmit and receive for LLDP(Link Layer Discovery Protocol) packets.
x-enumDescriptions.RX_ONLY:
Enable interface to only receive LLDP(Link Layer Discovery Protocol)
packets.LLDP packets will not be transmitted
x-enumDescriptions.TX_ONLY:
Enable interface to only transmit LLDP(Link Layer Discovery Protocol)
packets.LLDP packets receive will not work
x-enumDescriptions.TX_RX: Enable interface to both transmit and receive LLDP(Link Layer Discovery Protocol) packets.

mode.x-typeDescription: LLDP operational mode on this interface.

tlv.description: Configure TLV parameters to advertise on the specified ports.

elin-addr.description: Configure ELIN (Emergency Location Information Number) address location to advertise.

zone.

top-change-notify.description: Enable the LLDP MED topology change notification.

trap.description: Enable SNMP traps for LLDP neighbor changes.

nd-snooping-profile.description: Name of the profile containing ND snooping configurations.

allocation-method.description:
Set the power allocation method. By default, power allocation
is automatic based on device usage.

assigned-class.description: Set the maximum POE class for any device on this interface.

cycle-timeout.description: Set the timeout value in units of seconds to control POE power delivery.

enabled.description: Enable or disable PoE in the ethernet interface.

lldp-detect.description:
Allow the link partner to specify power allocation using LLDP.
By default, LLDP power information is ignored.

pd-class-override.description: Ignore the power class requested by the device and always use the assigned class.

x-enumDescriptions.ALT_A: Delivers power only on the ALT-A pair.
x-enumDescriptions.ALT_A_AND_B: Delivers power on the ALT-A and ALT-B pairs.

power-pairs.x-typeDescription: Configure what cable pairs deliver power.

pre-std-detect.description: Enable detection of pre-standard PoE devices.

value.description:
Set the maximum power allocation for the port.
Applicable only when 'poe-allocate-by' is set to 'value'.

ArubaUfd_UfdSchemaGET.description:
Uplink Failure Detection(UFD) Configurations.UFD monitors (tracks the forwarding state of)
the interfaces/LAGs(Link Aggregation) configured as links-to-monitor (LtM) and when all
LtM links go down,UFD disables the interfaces/LAGs configured as links-to-disable (LtD).
If any of the LtM links come back up, then all the LtD links are brought back up

profile.description: List of profiles for Uplink Failure Detection.

sessions.description: List of sessions for Uplink Failure Detection.

delay-down.description: Time to wait before disabling the links to disable ports.

delay-up.description: Time to wait before enabling the links to disable ports.

links-to-disable.description:
Configure links to disable.When all links-to-monitor goes down,
UFD disables the interfaces/LAGs configured as links-to-disable.

links-to-monitor.description:
Configure links to monitor.UFD monitors all the interfaces/LAGs
configured under links-to-monitor.

ArubaVrrpInterface_VrrpInterfaceConfigur.description: Grouping for IPv4 and IPv6 VRRP Interface Configurations

address.description: IPv4 or IPv6 address of the virtual router

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

items.description: Configure BFD peer IPs
items.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

ciphertext-password.description: Authentication password in cipher text (for IPv4 only)

cluster-preempt.description: Cluster manager drives the VRRP state (applicable only for IPv4 address family)

description.description: Description of the VRRP router instance

failback.description: Attempt to failback to VR when it is not enabled.

failover.description: Attempt to failover to VR when it is not enabled.

hold-time.description: Hold-time interval (state machine vrrp starts listening to advertisements after holdtime expires; applicable only when preempt is disabled or preempt-delay is 0)

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

password.description: Authentication password (applicable only for IPv4 address family)
password.x-typeDescription:
A custom derived type for special use cases. This type takes a plaintext password and uses
Vault's 3rd party API to encrypt the password. The intended use case is to encrypt and store
customer credentials which will be used later to login to 3rd party services.

preempt-delay.description: Router pre-emption delay (time to wait before becoming master)

router-id-address-family.description: Key combination of router-id and address-family separated by a tilde symbol, e.g., 1~IPV4

items.description: List of secondary IPv4 or IPv6 addresses.

oneOf.x-typeDescription:
An IPv4 address in dotted quad notation using the default
zone.

An IPv6 address represented as either a full address; shortened
or mixed-shortened formats, using the default zone.

items.description: Track object ID

version.description: VRRP version. VRRP version can not be set to VRRP_V2 for address family IPV6

version-3-checksum.description:
Enable VRRPv3 IPv4 checksum calculation.
VRRP version should be V3 when version-3-checksum is set

with-monitoring.description: Continue monitor of main VR during failover.

tracking-items.description: List for VRRP Tracking Configurations to Alter the Virtual Router's Priority Value

identifier.description: Virtual router identifier to track MASTER state of another VRRP master

interfaces.description: Gigabit ethernet interface to track the port's state

interval.description: Master elapsed time in minutes before tracking takes effect

mode.description: Tracking type to alter the virtual router's priority value (Tracking mode 'uplink' available only for IPv4 address family)

x-enumDescriptions.MASTER_STATE: Tracking based on another vrrp's MASTER state
x-enumDescriptions.MASTER_UPTIME: Tracking based on how long we have been MASTER
x-enumDescriptions.PORT: Tracking based on layer 2 interface state transition(s)
x-enumDescriptions.VLAN: Tracking based on vlan state transition(s)

priority-offset-op.description: Priority offset operation, to add or to subtract virtual router's priority

x-enumDescriptions.ADD: To add value to the virtual router's priority (applicable for MASTER_UPTIME and MASTER_STATE tracking types)
x-enumDescriptions.SUBTRACT: To subtract value from the virtual router's priority when link goes down (applicable for VLAN, PORT and UPLINK tracking types)

priority-offset-op.x-typeDescription: Operation to alter the value of virtual router's priority

uplink-vlan.description: Uplink VLAN (This must be already created or must exist under Uplink list configuration)
uplink-vlan.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

vlans.description: VLAN identifier to use for tracking changes
vlans.x-typeDescription: Type definition representing a single-tagged VLAN

ArubaWlanSecurity_AuthServerConfigGet.description: Group for Radius accounting related configurations.

auth-server-group.description: Server group to be used for authentication under the SSID profile or wired port profile.

backup-auth-server.description: Backup server for authentication under SSID profile or wired port profile.

primary-auth-server.description: Primary server for authentication under SSID profile or wired port profile.

default-role.description: When Default role is configured, default role is used. If default role isn't configured, use the role that the role name is same as SSID profile name.

mac-auth-only-role.description:
Allow administrator to create a MAC Authentication only role for role-based access rule when MAC authentication is enabled for Captive Portal.
If MAC authentication is successful, the mac-auth-only role will be assigned to the client.

machine-auth.description:
Configure a machine authentication rule. When a Windows device boots, it logs onto the network domain using a machine account. Within the domain, the device is authenticated
before computer group policies and software settings can be executed; this process is known as machine authentication. Machine authentication ensures that only authorized devices
are allowed on the network. You can configure 802.1X for both user and machine authentication. This tightens the authentication process further, since both the device and user need
to be authenticated. This feature is applicable for 802.1x authenticaion and it is only applicable for AP.

machine-only-role.description: Client can obtain machine auth role, when it only pass machine authentication. Assign different rights to clients based on whether their hardware device supports machine authentication.

user-only-role.description: Client can obtain user only role, when using username and password pass authentication.

pre-auth-role.description: Configure a pre-authentication role to allow some access to the guest users before the client authentication.

default-role.description: When Default role is configured, default role is used. If default role isn't configured, use the role that the role name is same as SSID profile name.

mac-auth-only-role.description:
Allow administrator to create a MAC Authentication only role for role-based access rule when MAC authentication is enabled for Captive Portal.
If MAC authentication is successful, the mac-auth-only role will be assigned to the client.

are allowed on the network. You can configure 802.1X for both user and machine authentication. This tightens the authentication process further, since both the device and user need
to be authenticated. This feature is applicable for 802.1x authenticaion and it is only applicable for AP.

user-only-role.description: Client can obtain user only role, when using username and password pass authentication.

ArubaWlanSecurity_RadiusAccountingConfig.description: Group for Radius accounting related configurations.

acct-server-group.description:
Server group to be used for accounting under the SSID profile or wired port profile. The primary/backup accounting
server and accounting server group only support one configuration at a time. If both are configured simultaneously,
the accounting server group will be used during accounting.

backup-acct-server.description: Backup accounting servers for radius accounting under the SSID profile or wired port profile.

cp-accounting-mode.description: Radius Accounting Mode just for captive portal.

x-enumDescriptions.USER_ASSOCIATION: When configured, the accounting starts when the client associates to the network successfully and stops when the client is disconnected.
x-enumDescriptions.USER_AUTHENTICATION: when configured, the accounting starts only after client authentication is successful and stops when the client logs out of the network.

cp-accounting-mode.x-typeDescription: Configure an accounting mode for the captive portal users.

primary-acct-server.description: Primary accounting servers for radius accounting under the SSID profile or wired port profile.

get.description: Ethernet interfaces provide physical connectivity for network devices including switches, gateways, access points, and bridges. This module supports configuration of interface properties, port profiles, link aggregation, Power over Ethernet (PoE), security features (AAA, port-security, LLDP), Layer 3 routing capabilities, and advanced features like PIM multicast, flow telemetry, and port monitoring. Supported scopes: Device. This configuration relies on Port Profile configurations (sw-profile for switches, gw-profile for gateways, ap-profile for access points, br-profile for bridges) for template-based settings. Interface names follow device-specific patterns (e.g., gateway model GW_7005 supports 0/0/[0-3], GW_7205 supports 0/0/[0-5]). Use this API to retrieve the list of Ethernet Interface configurations.
get.summary: Read list of Ethernet Interface configurations

get.description: Represents a single physical Ethernet or USB interface configuration on the device. Each interface is uniquely identified by its name (e.g., 0/0/1, enet0, usb0). Use these APIs to create, modify, or delete an interface configuration instance.

parameters.description: Unique identifier for the Ethernet or USB interface. Supported formats include slot-based naming (e.g., 0/0/1 for slot 0, module 0, port 1) and logical naming (e.g., enet0, usb0). Device models enforce specific port ranges through capability constraints (e.g., gateway model GW_7005 supports 0/0/[0-3], GW_7205 supports 0/0/[0-5]). This field is mandatory and serves as the primary key for the interface instance.
parameters.name: name

get.summary: Read existing Ethernet Interface instance configuration

get.description:
To configure and manage port profiles. A port-profile can be used to configure
ethernet interface. Same profile can be applied to multiple interfaces and
avoids repeating same configuration for interfaces. Gigabit ethernet config, LLDP,
spanning-tree, switch-port configuration, etc can be applied using the port-profile.

get.description:
List of Interface Profile configuration. Interface Profile is bundle of interface configuration
for devices with specific sku/model. Defined for set of uplink/downlink ports for given models.
Interface Profile can be defined for stacks/chassis for fixed/automatic set of members/LineCards.
Interface Profile can be defined for custom stack/chassis size.
One Interface Profile can have port-profile config for uplink + downlink + custom ports for given
model/device type. In case of stacks, Interface Profile will not be applied VSF link ports, if they
are part of automatic/custom Interface Profile model+ports.

delete.description:

are part of automatic/custom Interface Profile model+ports.

delete.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.
delete.summary: Delete existing LACP configuration at the system level

get.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.
get.summary: Read existing configuration at the system level

patch.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.
patch.summary: Modify LACP configuration at the system level

post.description:
Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) dynamically negotiates and
manages link aggregation groups (LAGs/Port Channels) per IEEE
802.3ad/802.1AX standards. LACP provides automatic failover, load
distribution across member links, and detection of misconfigured
aggregation. Configure system-level LACP priority, hashing algorithms,
and interface-specific parameters including active/passive modes,
heartbeat rates (fast/slow), and minimum active link thresholds. Use
this API to retrieve LACP system configuration.
post.summary: Create LACP configuration at the system level

get.description:
The Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) is an industry-standard,
vendor-neutral method to allow networked devices to advertise capabilities,
discover and identify other LLDP enabled devices and gather information in a
LAN. LLDP is supported on physical interfaces and Out-Of-Band Management (OOBM)
interfaces and it is not supported on logical interfaces.

get.description:
Mirror-Endpoints terminate a remote mirror tunnel. They serve to control packet
decapsulation and forwarding to the mirror destinations.
Creating a Mirror-Endpoint is independent of the corresponding mirror session,
which means there is no ordering requirement for the creation of a mirror session
and a Mirror Endpoint.

get.description:
Mirror Configuration. Mirroring is the ability of a switch to
transmit a copy of a packet out another system interface. This allows
network administrators to replicate all traffic arriving and/or leaving
selected system interfaces for collection/analysis purposes.

get.description:
Port Channel (Link Aggregation Group/LAG) interfaces combine multiple
physical Ethernet links into a single logical interface for increased
bandwidth, load balancing, and redundancy per IEEE 802.3ad. Port
channels support static aggregation and dynamic LACP negotiation with
features including VLAN trunking, routing protocols, access control,
and QoS policies. Configure port channel member interfaces, LACP
parameters, VLAN assignments, IP addressing, and link protection
settings. Supported scopes: Device. Device Limits: Gateway supports
port channels 0-7 (8 total). Use this API to retrieve the list of
port channel interfaces.

delete.description:
Port channel interface aggregating multiple physical Ethernet ports
into a single logical trunk. Each entity is identified by portchannel
ID. Configure member ports, LACP parameters, VLANs, IP addresses,
routing protocols, and port-security settings. Device Limits: Gateway
supports port channels 0-7. Use these APIs to create, modify, or
delete a port channel interface.

get.description:

patch.description:
Port channel interface aggregating multiple physical Ethernet ports

get.description:
Configure and manage port profiles.
A port profile is a template for configuring ethernet interface configurations.
It is a mechanism intended to ease the process of configuring multiple interfaces
with same set of configurations. Instead of manually configuring each interface one-by-one,
the port profile collects the common configuration settings and allows them to be applied on
the interface or a collection of interfaces.

get.description:
Uplink Failure Detection(UFD) Configurations.UFD monitors (tracks the forwarding state of)
the interfaces/LAGs(Link Aggregation) configured as links-to-monitor (LtM) and when all
LtM links go down,UFD disables the interfaces/LAGs configured as links-to-disable (LtD).
If any of the LtM links come back up, then all the LtD links are brought back up

get.description:
Layer 3 VLAN (Switched Virtual Interface/SVI) configurations provide
IP routing functionality for VLANs, enabling inter-VLAN routing and
gateway services. VLAN interfaces support IPv4/IPv6 addressing, DHCP
relay, routing protocol participation (OSPF, BGP, RIP), VRRP for high
availability, and access control policies. Configure VLAN interface IP
addresses, DHCP servers/relay agents, multicast protocols, routing
parameters, and security policies. Device Limits: Gateway supports max
3 IPv6 addresses, 16 IPv4 helper addresses, and 3 IPv6 helper
addresses per VLAN interface. Use this API to retrieve the list of
VLAN interfaces.

delete.description:
Layer 3 VLAN interface (SVI) enabling IP routing for a VLAN segment.
Each entity is identified by VLAN ID. Configure IP addresses
(IPv4/IPv6), DHCP services, routing protocol parameters, VRRP, active
gateway, and access policies for inter-VLAN routing. Device Limits:
Gateway supports max 3 IPv6 addresses, 16 IPv4 helper addresses, and 3
IPv6 helper addresses per VLAN interface. Use these APIs to create,
modify, or delete a VLAN interface.

get.description:
